Bumping `MenuBarExtraAccess` to 1.3.0 is unsafe because the new version requires `menuBarExtraAccess(...)` to be the first modifier on `MenuBarExtra`, but `MenuBar.swift` still applies `.menuBarExtraStyle(.menu)` first, which can break status-item initialization and menu/session behavior at runtime.

The `hasPayloadText` check incorrectly counts reasoning-only payloads as valid user-facing content, causing 429/overload error rewrites to be skipped and leaving reasoning-enabled turns with no visible reply after reasoning payloads are dropped during delivery.

The smkx/rmkx detection logic only inspects the current PTY chunk without buffering carry-over bytes, so if the escape sequence `\x1b[?1h` or `\x1b[?1l` is split across two chunks, it will never be recognized and `session.cursorKeyMode` will remain stale for the rest of the session, causing arrow keys to be encoded incorrectly.

The new try/catch around `resolveMediaList` silently swallows unexpected resolver errors as verbose-only log entries instead of surfacing them as worker failure signals, causing malformed payloads or future regressions to silently drop media in non-verbose deployments rather than triggering the existing error-handling infrastructure.

The cleanup command hardcodes the resource group name `rg-openclaw` instead of using the configured `${RG}` variable, which could cause users who deployed with a custom resource group name to either fail to clean up their resources (leaving unexpected costs) or accidentally delete the wrong resource group.

The extension-based fast path for groupability checks bypasses MIME validation, causing mislabeled or redirecting media URLs to be incorrectly forced into `sendMediaGroup` as photo/video instead of falling back to the appropriate send method, which can break entire reply delivery.

The `Promise.race`-based timeout in `resolveScriptTagline` cannot interrupt synchronous blocking work in the imported script, because the event loop is blocked and the timer callback never fires, potentially hanging the CLI indefinitely.

The refactored early-return logic now silently discards all non-matching webhook messages in bound threads before they reach the `allowBots` check, causing any third-party webhook messages in those threads to be incorrectly ignored instead of dispatched.

When creating a new chat session, `switchChatSession()` fails to clear `state.chatAttachments`, causing attachments from the previous session to persist into the new session where they can be accidentally uploaded to the wrong conversation.

The regex in `stripStructuralPrefixes` only removes `---`/`Queued #N` headers but fails to strip overflow summary blocks prepended when the queue exceeds its cap, causing `isAbortRequestText()` to still miss `/stop` commands in the high-backlog scenarios where collect mode is most commonly triggered.

The zero-usage branch in `persistSessionUsageUpdate()` unconditionally clears `totalTokens` and `totalTokensFresh` without backfilling `totalTokensEstimate`, causing sessions migrated from older stores to permanently lose their last known context size and display as "unknown" in status and UI views.

The `parseAgentSessionKey` function silently accepts malformed session keys containing empty path segments (e.g., `agent:main::`), which pass the `classifySessionKeyShape` validation check and can cause voice-wake routes to silently create or resolve the wrong session instead of rejecting the bad input.

The new `!raw.startsWith("![")` guard is overly broad and incorrectly routes valid shell commands like `![ -f .env ] && echo yes` to chat instead of the local shell handler, breaking common `[`/`test` syntax for TUI operators.

Pre-reserving every unique basename causes all workspace symlinks to incorrectly trigger the collision-deduplication branch, renaming non-colliding entries like `/repos/api` to `<parent>-api` instead of `api` and producing unstable, unpredictable composite link names.

When `--workspace` is omitted from CLI sync commands, the configured `agents.defaults.workspace` path is ignored and sync operations silently run against the wrong default directory, leaving the actual agent workspace unmodified.

The DuckDuckGo client uses a direct `fetch` call instead of the shared trusted web endpoint wrapper (`withTrustedWebSearchEndpoint`/`withTrustedWebToolsEndpoint`), bypassing the standard proxy/guarded networking path and centralized outbound guardrails used by all other web-search providers, which can cause failures in proxy-restricted deployments.

The plugin manifest schema blocks `provider` and `outputDimensionality` fields with `additionalProperties: false`, even though the runtime config parser supports them, causing valid embedding options to be silently rejected before the plugin ever executes.

Because `applyModelDefaults()` always pre-populates `model.contextWindow` before this code runs, the new `options.num_ctx` user configuration is silently ignored in favor of the default context window value, making the intended override logic unreachable.

Adding "querit" to the bundled web search ID list without updating the corresponding provider lookup map causes `resolveBundledWebSearchPluginId("querit")` to return `undefined`, breaking production code that relies on this helper to map Querit back to its plugin ID.

The vision support check uses the default model to decide whether to populate image attachments before model overrides are applied, meaning the final model that actually runs may be mismatched — either missing images it supports or receiving images it doesn't support.

`hasStoredLocalOperatorDeviceAuthToken()` does not validate that the stored token matches the current device identity, so a stale token left over from a regenerated `identity/device.json` can incorrectly suppress the normal-closure pairing fallback, causing `devices approve` to fail with `gateway closed (1000)` on loopback restarts.

A guard that prevents overwriting the active plugin registry allows a stale registry to persist after plugin configuration changes, causing `config.schema` to silently omit channel entries for newly configured plugins until a restart.

The new `read_config_gateway_token` implementation runs `openclaw-cli` via Docker Compose before volume mounts and image builds are configured, preventing it from accessing the host config file and causing a new gateway token to be generated on every rerun when `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN` is unset, which can unexpectedly invalidate existing clients.

The mermaid fence rewriting logic converts all code blocks to `.mermaid-wrapper` placeholders globally, but the `renderMermaidInContainer()` function that resolves them to SVGs is only invoked in `.chat-thread` contexts, causing mermaid snippets rendered via other callers like `markdown-sidebar.ts` and `agents-panels-status-files.ts` to display as broken placeholder UI instead of diagrams or plain code blocks.

The global transport state only tracks a single `activeLogger`, so any child loggers (e.g. per-channel or plugin loggers) constructed before the OTEL diagnostics service starts will never have the OTLP transport late-attached, causing their logs to be silently dropped from OpenTelemetry in multi-channel and plugin setups.

In `resolvePromptBuildHookResult`, `sessionKey` and `agentId` are unconditionally added to the legacy hook event object even when undefined, breaking compatibility with legacy plugins that check for property presence and violating existing test assertions that expect only `{ prompt, messages }`.

Making `appPrincipal` required in the Zod schema without updating the guided setup flow to collect it will cause the Google Chat configuration to fail validation and prevent saving during the setup wizard.

When syncing a new auth profile credential to sibling agent directories, the helper fails to update each sibling's `store.order`, causing the newly synced profile to be invisible on any sibling that has a local auth-order override, since `resolveAuthProfileOrder()` prioritizes `store.order` over config.

The new regex pattern incorrectly matches Telegram's "bot was blocked by the user" 403 error, causing it to be rewritten as a misleading "bot is not a member of the chat" error message and sending operators down the wrong troubleshooting path.

When upgrading legacy sessions that have `totalTokens` but no `totalTokensEstimate`, the new code fails to seed `totalTokensEstimate` from the existing cached value because it clears `totalTokens` and only writes `totalTokensEstimate` from the current result, which is `undefined` in the very cases it's meant to preserve — causing those sessions to lose their cached token count until a fresh prompt snapshot is taken.

Adding `rawBody` to the `FollowupRun` type is insufficient because the `queue.mode=collect` drain path reconstructs a new `FollowupRun` without `rawBody`, causing it to be `undefined` when multiple messages are batched and leaving plugin hooks without the clean user input text this change intended to expose.

The newly added `replyToId` and `mentionedJids` fields are never read by the underlying `sendMessage` runtime, so extensions passing reply/mention metadata through the new signature will silently send plain messages instead of quotes or @mentions.

When `OPENCLAW_HOME` is changed or unset after installation, `resolveTaskScriptCandidates()` fails to locate the originally installed script path, causing `stop`/`restart` commands and Startup-folder relaunches to target the wrong script and potentially leave the actual gateway process running.

When an account is deleted or renamed during the drain window of a channel hot reload, `postDrainStatus` becomes `undefined` but the code still calls `startChannel`, potentially resurrecting a removed account using stale base config settings instead of honoring the updated configuration.

After a successful reconnect, in-flight retry attempts in `deliverWebReply()` are not woken from their backoff sleep because `sleepWithAbort()` only listens for terminal shutdown signals, causing up to 30+ seconds of unnecessary reply latency even though the new socket is already ready.

The `validateGatewayTailscaleControlUrl` function incorrectly rejects the official Tailscale control URL (e.g., `https://controlplane.tailscale.com`) in serve/funnel mode by treating any non-empty `controlUrl` as custom infrastructure, breaking configs that explicitly set the default and contradicting the existing `isOfficialTailscaleControlServer()` helper in `server-runtime-config.ts`.

Switching the `--json` output from filtered `paired` entries to `NodeListNode` objects silently drops pairing metadata fields (`lastConnectedAtMs`, `createdAtMs`, `approvedAtMs`), breaking any automation that relies on those timestamps from `openclaw nodes list --json`.

`getPluginMediaProviders()` is called without passing the `cfg` configuration object, causing it to run with `config: undefined` and potentially activating a different plugin registry that drops config-scoped providers from capability-based discovery.

The `looksLikeShortProfileId` function rejects alphanumeric-only validation, causing Bedrock Application Inference Profile IDs containing hyphens (e.g., `my-claude-profile`) to bypass the Claude-name fallback and be incorrectly treated as non-Anthropic models, leaving prompt caching disabled for those profiles.

Although the timeout budget is now correctly passed to `resolveProbeBudgetMs`, `probeGateway()` constructs `GatewayClient` without forwarding `requestTimeoutMs`, so internal RPCs still default to 30,000ms and will ignore any user-specified timeout greater than that.

The new assertion that no "duplicate plugin id" diagnostic exists is applied unconditionally to all test scenarios, but certain scenarios (e.g., "bundled beats auto-discovered global duplicate") are expected to emit that warning, which will cause false test failures and block the change.

In the manual Entra fallback, `modelNameHint` is always set to the deployment ID instead of the actual model name, causing `resolveFoundryApi` to misroute GPT-5 deployments with custom names (e.g., `prod-chat`) to the wrong endpoint family.

The `hasToolCalls` check incorrectly relies on an ID-based extraction helper that returns false for valid tool calls missing an `id` field, meaning tool-call-only assistant responses from providers that omit IDs will still cause `handleMessageEnd` to silently skip emitting the assistant update, leaving the downstream idle/no-progress bug unfixed.

The media registry cache key only considers plugin-registry version and ignores the `cfg` argument, allowing different configs to incorrectly reuse a stale cached registry and potentially miss provider or capability changes until an unrelated version bump occurs.

Switching from a pre-resolved `CONFIG_DIR` constant to a lazily-called `resolveConfigDir()` causes managed skills to be resolved against the wrong working directory when `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` is a relative path and `process.chdir()` is called before skill loading, silently dropping all managed skills.

The devcontainer's `postCreateCommand` runs `npm install` instead of `pnpm install`, immediately bootstrapping developers into a broken state that the project's own doctor script explicitly warns against, since this is a pnpm workspace that requires pnpm for a valid install.

In DM flows where `materialize()` is bypassed (e.g., media payloads, inline buttons, or oversize text), the streamed draft message is never cleared because `clear()` only removes messages with a `message_id`, resulting in a stale draft bubble persisting alongside the actual final message.

The DeepInfra wrapper incorrectly injects `reasoning.effort` for non-thinking models (e.g., `Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-480B-A35B-Instruct-Turbo`) because the reasoning suppression logic only targets `x-ai/*` models, potentially causing requests to be rejected or ignored by the API.

Changing the `hasNonzeroUsage` predicate from `v > 0` to `v >= 0` causes all-zero usage records to be treated as valid nonzero usage, which can cause `runMemoryFlushIfNeeded()` to latch onto a zero-valued trailing transcript entry instead of the real prior API call's usage snapshot, potentially preventing memory compaction from triggering even when the prompt is near the threshold.

The auto-thread fallback unconditionally copies `toolContext.currentThreadTs` into `replyToId` regardless of whether the destination Mattermost channel matches the originating thread's channel, causing cross-context sends to use a mismatched `rootId` and breaking posts to other Mattermost targets.

The refactored `sanitizeHostExecEnvWithDiagnostics()` inadvertently drops non-portable Windows environment variables like `ProgramFiles(x86)` by rebuilding the base environment through `listNormalizedPortableEnvEntries()`, potentially breaking commands and toolchains that rely on those variables to locate SDKs or installers.

The XML closing-tag suffix stripping is applied to all string parameters indiscriminately, which can silently corrupt legitimate file content in write/edit operations where fields like `content`, `oldText`, or `newText` may validly end with `</arg_value>` — the fix should scope the sanitization only to known affected fields like command and path arguments.

Bundled providers like `byteplus`, `volcengine`, and `google-gemini-cli` are missing from `BUILTIN_PROVIDER_IDS`, causing `isCustomProvider()` to incorrectly return true for first-party catalog entries and `sortRankedChatModelOptions()` to incorrectly sort them above genuine built-in providers.

In `resolveChoiceContext`, both the authorization and choice context resolution paths independently resolve thread metadata, causing redundant network/cache lookups on every autocomplete keystroke that can push response times over Discord's deadline and result in dropped or delayed suggestions.

The context window cache uses only the model name as its key, so querying the same model name from two different Ollama server instances can return a stale, incorrect context window size for up to 5 minutes, potentially causing invalid requests to be sent to the second server.

The patch fails to populate `options.dispatcher` in the Baileys socket config, causing media downloads and other fetch-based requests (e.g., `downloadMediaMessage` via `getHttpStream`) to bypass the proxy and fail in firewall-restricted environments, even though `fetchAgent` is set.

Adding `GOOGLE_CLOUD_API_KEY` to the `google-vertex` auth candidates will cause authentication to silently fail in shell-fallback environments because `loadShellEnvFallback()` does not include this key in its import list, meaning the variable is never copied from the user's login shell into the process environment.

The proxy setup in `createWaSocket()` unconditionally routes all WhatsApp traffic through the proxy whenever an HTTPS proxy environment variable is set, ignoring `NO_PROXY`/`no_proxy` exclusion rules and breaking deployments that intentionally bypass the proxy for WhatsApp hosts.

The new provider-inference path only searches `agents.defaults.models` entries but ignores `agents.defaults.model.fallbacks`, so bare model names that are exclusively allowlisted via fallbacks under a non-default provider still fail resolution with "model not allowed" even though those fallbacks are treated as valid selections elsewhere in the codebase.

Wrapping `agent.streamFn` with `createOpenAIDefaultTransportWrapper` a second time in `extra-params.ts` causes the outer wrapper's default transport settings to override any model-level extra params (e.g., `transport: "sse"` or `openaiWsWarmup: true`) that are applied afterward, preventing direct OpenAI users from configuring transport behavior through config.

The `await resolveGatewayProbeAuthResolution()` call executes unconditionally before the early-exit checks for `remoteUrlMissing` or `skipProbe`, meaning an asynchronous secret resolution failure can crash the status command even when no gateway probe is ever attempted.

Setting `totalTokensFresh = true` before checking `if (totalTokens > 0 || !cronSession.sessionEntry.totalTokensFresh)` means a fresh zero-token snapshot can never overwrite a stale `totalTokensEstimate`, causing session status to incorrectly revert to the old pre-reset context size instead of reflecting the actual zero.

Redundantly remocking the already-mocked `src/auto-reply/dispatch` module overwrites the `inboundCtxCapture` sink, causing Discord (and potentially Signal) dispatch calls to bypass context capture and making those contract test assertions non-deterministic or broken.

The patch for `@mariozechner/pi-ai` is only applied in the local pnpm workspace and is not included in the published npm package, meaning users who install `openclaw` globally via npm will still be affected by the signature-rejection bug.

When inferring a provider from a bare model name like "claude-sonnet-4-6", passing only the parsed model ID to `inferUniqueProviderFromConfiguredModels()` strips upstream gateway prefixes (e.g., "vercel-ai-gateway/anthropic/"), causing the lookup to fail and incorrectly rejecting a uniquely allowlisted model in gateway/openrouter setups.

An unguarded call to `inspectBundleMcpRuntimeSupport()` during plugin loading can throw on malformed bundle MCP config (e.g. broken `.mcp.json`), causing the entire `loadOpenClawPlugins()` function to abort instead of gracefully downgrading the error to a plugin diagnostic.

The inbound chat debounce guard fails to account for active tool-stream state, allowing `loadChatHistory` to be called mid-run, which resets tool streaming state via `maybeResetToolStream` and causes in-progress tool cards to disappear until repopulated by later events.

The resize-handle hover code pushes `NSCursor` without a corresponding `pop()` on view disappearance, which can leave the resize cursor permanently stuck if the window closes or the view is replaced while the pointer is still hovering over the handle.

The non-interactive onboarding command example in the Fireworks setup documentation is missing the required `--accept-risk` flag, which will cause the command to immediately fail when users copy and run it.

The `createOllamaThinkingPayloadWrapper` is incorrectly applied for every `thinkingLevel === "off"` run (which is also the default), sending an unsupported boolean `think=false` to Ollama's GPT-OSS model instead of the required `low|medium|high` values, making it impossible to disable reasoning in that supported configuration.

When the recovery time budget is exhausted, the deadline-exceeded branch fails to check `MAX_RETRIES` before processing remaining entries, allowing deliveries that have already hit the retry limit to persist in `pending/` indefinitely instead of being archived to `failed/`.

When a superseded in-flight preview send resolves after `forceNewMessage()` has advanced the generation, the snapshot fields `lastSentText`/`lastSentParseMode` are unconditionally overwritten with stale data, causing downstream deduplication and materialization logic to operate on the wrong generation's content.

The predicate that decides whether to inject a `default` account only checks for inline token fields and ignores `tokenFile`-based accounts, causing the base `default` provider to never be started when a Zalo account is configured via `tokenFile` alongside a top-level bot token.

The cooldown logic for exhausted auth profiles is a no-op in the premature-resolution branch because `lastAssistant.errorMessage` is empty at that point, causing `classifyFailoverReason` to return null and leaving the exhausted credential eligible for reuse on the next turn in multi-profile setups.

The Windows gateway PID finder incorrectly matches any Node.js process on the target port instead of restricting matches to OpenClaw processes, risking termination of unrelated Node applications during stale PID cleanup.

The `resolveSlackThreadContextData` function passes raw envelope timezone mode keywords (e.g., `local`, `host`, `user`) directly to `buildImageAnnotation`, which expects valid IANA timezone strings, causing it to silently fall back to UTC and producing incorrect annotation timestamps that can mislead the model's recency reasoning.

When no preview URL is provided, `previewImageUrl` incorrectly defaults to the video URL itself, causing LINE API rejections because video messages require the preview URL to point to an image, not a video file.

The `rust_random_bytes` tool incorrectly calls `nativeAddon.randomBytes`, which does not exist in the native addon — the correct exported function is `secureRandom` — causing a `TypeError` at runtime on first use.

The per-agent `contextTokens` override is only applied when it is lower than the base token count, so agents cannot use their configuration to raise or disable a stricter global default, leaving them artificially capped despite having a higher override value.

The new `!command.commandBodyNormalized.startsWith("![")` guard intended to exclude Markdown image syntax is overly broad and incorrectly blocks valid bash commands like `![ -f package.json ] && echo ok`, breaking the documented `!` alias for shell `[`/`test` expressions.

The `withTimeout` helper rejects the promise after the specified duration but does not cancel the underlying async operation, allowing timed-out memory searches to continue running in the background and potentially causing a buildup of overlapping in-flight requests under a slow or unresponsive memory backend.

The standalone `src/acp/server.ts` entrypoint calls `routeLogsToStderr()` without first calling `enableConsoleCapture()`, meaning `console.log`/`console.info` are never actually redirected to stderr and can still corrupt the NDJSON stdout stream.

The example implementation hardcodes `DISCOVERY_PLATFORM_URL` to a placeholder value instead of reading it from environment configuration, causing service registration to fail at the wrong URL unless users manually edit the source code.

When `NU_VERSION` is set inside a bash/zsh login shell, `getShellConfig()` now executes commands with `nu -c` while `detectRuntimeShell()` still reports the login shell, causing the model to generate bash/zsh syntax that will fail under nushell's incompatible command semantics.

When switching from GigaChat Basic to OAuth onboarding, the old Basic endpoint URL is retained in the provider config instead of being reset to the correct OAuth host, causing all subsequent OAuth requests to fail until the user manually edits their configuration.

Hard-coding `compat.supportsStore = false` for onboarded Azure custom models makes the Azure Responses compaction path permanently unreachable, preventing `context_management` injection and regressing large-context session handling compared to the supported Azure Responses flow.

Adding both `"text/xml": ".xml"` and `"application/xml": ".xml"` to `EXT_BY_MIME` causes the reversed `MIME_BY_EXT` map to overwrite the `text/xml` entry with `application/xml`, so any `.xml` file will resolve to the wrong MIME type in `detectMime()`.

The catch block in `resolvePluginSdkRuntimeImportHref` indiscriminately swallows all `require.resolve` errors and falls back to importing source files directly, causing the test to silently pass even when a subpath is genuinely broken in the published package (e.g., due to a missing or misconfigured export in `package.json`), giving false confidence that plugin consumers won't hit runtime import failures.

When an OpenRouter model reference like `openrouter/healer-alpha` is resolved via the new alternate stripped-ID lookup, the returned model loses its required `openrouter/` prefix, producing an invalid API model ID that breaks OpenRouter-native requests.

The `movj` tool hard-codes a 12-second reply timeout that doesn't account for actual motion duration, causing timeout errors for valid slow or large-angle joint movements that the viewer correctly executes over longer periods.

The `context_assembled` hook exposes the live `imageResult.images` array without cloning it, meaning any plugin that mutates `event.images` during the hook will nondeterministically corrupt the actual vision payload sent to the model.
When an inline query times out, the in-flight `llmPromise` is never cancelled, causing expensive abandoned LLM runs and unnecessary session writes to accumulate silently in the background.

The `fetchJson` function in `warroom.ts` makes HTTP requests to SCid and Ollama without an `AbortSignal` timeout, meaning a stalled upstream service can cause the requests to hang indefinitely and block agent turns instead of failing fast.

The mirrored reply to the external channel is awaited on the critical path of the webchat response, meaning a slow or failed Telegram/Discord send will block the browser turn from completing even though the mirror is intended to be best-effort.

When `preferResolvedModel()` merges a dynamic model over a discovered one, it never restores the discovered `contextWindow`, causing downstream prompt sizing to budget against the larger template window (1.05M tokens) instead of the backend's actual lower limit, which can result in oversized requests for proxy-backed Codex installs.

The `hasMonitorGroups()` function only checks the top-level `cfg.channels.whatsapp.groups` config path and ignores per-account group configurations, causing named accounts with `requireMention: "monitor"` set under their account-specific groups to never enable history sync or community participant fetching in a multi-account deployment.

When `OPENCLAW_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS` is set above 10 seconds, client-side RPC commands will still time out prematurely because `callGateway()` and multiple CLI entry points hardcode a 10-second transport timeout that isn't updated to match the new server-side handshake budget.

The new `cdpPort` field for targeting a specific Chrome instance in `existing-session` profiles is not propagated through the profile creation API, `CreateProfileParams`, or the CLI, meaning profiles created via these supported paths will always fall back to auto-discovering an arbitrary Chrome instance instead of using the intended port.

A fire-and-forget `mirrorWebchatTextToTarget` call may complete after `dispatchInboundMessage` has already processed the operator's message, causing recipients in the external channel to see the bot's response before the operator message that triggered it, breaking message ordering in the bidirectional sync flow.

In oneshot ACP sessions, if the direct child process exits with code 0 but leaves a detached subprocess (e.g., Codex/Claude) running, the guard condition that checks for abort or a still-running child will skip `killProcessTree()`, leaking the orphaned process since the "preserve warm owner" rationale doesn't apply when the session is immediately closed after completion.

The `agent_call` function ignores `SkillDeclaration.timeoutSeconds` and always falls back to a hard-coded 60-second timeout when the caller omits `timeoutSeconds`, rendering the per-skill default configuration field ineffective and causing long-running skills to prematurely return `working`.

The `looksLikeShortProfileId` regex only permits lowercase alphanumerics and hyphens, causing Bedrock Application Inference Profile IDs containing valid characters like `.` or `:` to be misclassified as non-Anthropic models, which incorrectly disables prompt caching.

The max-length check in `sendOrEditStreamMessage` incorrectly uses `renderedText` instead of the effective plain-text fallback (`effectiveText`), which can cause valid messages to be falsely flagged as exceeding 4096 characters after HTML parse-mode degradation, incorrectly halting the stream and delivering a truncated or stale final message.

The reordered candidate list causes `session_status("main")` to incorrectly resolve to the stale legacy key `agent:main:main` before the live alias `global`, potentially attaching the status card and model overrides to the wrong session entry when both keys coexist in the store.

The capability validation logic only checks the primary model or override instead of all configured fallbacks, causing valid image generation requests to be incorrectly rejected before the runtime fallback chain can attempt them with a compatible provider.

The thread-inheritance fallback silently fails for manual or embedded Mattermost runs because `to` is normalized to a canonical form (e.g., `channel:<id>`) before `handleAction()` runs while `currentChannelId` retains the raw original value (e.g., `#channel` or a bare ID), causing the exact equality check to never match even when both refer to the same conversation.

The webchat mirror receives UI-cleaned text that has already had inline reply tags (e.g., `[[reply_to_current]]`) stripped by `resolveBufferedChatTextState`, causing threaded assistant replies from webchat sessions to fall back to plain top-level posts on the external channel instead of being correctly threaded.

When `OPENCLAW_HOME` points to a legacy-named state directory, the nesting guard causes `CONFIG_PATH` to remain stale and misaligned with the path actually used for config I/O, so operations like `openclaw onboard --reset config` will delete the wrong file and leave the active config intact.

When `envelopeMode` is false, `openbrain_capture` always calls MCP with only `{"content": finalContent}`, silently dropping `category`, `tags`, and `source` metadata instead of forwarding them as structured arguments, causing permanent metadata loss on servers that support native metadata fields.

Calling `clearPluginCommands()` at the start of each reload now wipes the process-global command registry immediately, causing any plugin commands that arrive during the rebuild—or after a partially failed reload—to go unmatched until the new registry is fully populated.

When a full Brave endpoint URL (e.g., `.../res/v1/web/search`) is provided as `baseUrl`, `resolveBraveEndpoint()` incorrectly appends the path segment again, producing a doubled and invalid URL like `.../res/v1/web/search/res/v1/web/search` that results in 404 errors.

Querit's `page_age` field returns full ISO timestamps (e.g., `2025-07-20T16:00:00Z`), but `normalizeToIsoDate` only handles bare dates, causing `published` to always be written as `undefined` and stripping recency signals from API responses.

The new style-suffix parser incorrectly strips trailing "primary" or "danger" segments from button callback values, causing click handlers to silently receive truncated payloads for any existing namespaced values that legitimately end in those keywords.

The image history rebuild logic only reads from `message.attachments` but ignores the `MediaPath`/`MediaPaths` + `MediaType` fields that the gateway actually uses to persist uploaded images, meaning page refreshes after sending an image will still render no image.

By passing `consumeReloadRepairSkip: false` in the post-run maintenance call, a reload-skip marker for a malformed job is never consumed under a continuously busy scheduler, causing `scheduleErrorCount` to stall at 1 indefinitely and preventing the job from ever reaching the 3-strike auto-disable/alert threshold.

The `normalizeStateDirEnv` function only canonicalizes state directory environment variables but omits `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, meaning subsequent `process.chdir()` calls can still cause `resolveConfigPath()` to resolve against a shifted working directory, leaving config-driven discovery vulnerable to the same path-drift issue this patch intends to fix.

The `querySmsMmsMessagesByPhone()` function omits the `status` column from its projection, causing all SMS messages in mixed by-phone searches to lose their real delivery state (pending/failed/delivered) and always report a default status of 0.

On Windows, `path.relative()` returns backslash-separated paths that never match the forward-slash entries in `LEGACY_ALLOWLIST`, causing all allowlisted offenders to appear as new violations and incorrectly failing the lint check.

The "already running" check uses `findstr` to match the English word "Running" in `schtasks` output, which will silently fail on non-English Windows installations where task status text is localized, defeating the duplicate-start prevention this code intends to provide.

The `isUnknownGroupPolicyBlock` function incorrectly classifies `group-policy-allowlist-empty` as an unknown group block even when the group is intentionally covered by a wildcard (`"*"`) allowlist entry, which could cause the bot to erroneously warn or leave groups that are legitimately configured.

The provider inference fallback uses a model string already normalized against the wrong default provider, causing allowlisted bare aliases like `sonnet-4.6` or `gemini-3-pro` to fail with "model not allowed" because their canonical IDs are provider-dependent and must be normalized per candidate rather than upfront.

The `sanitizeUserFacingText` function's generic `model_not_found` rewrite strips out the original error's concrete model identifier and recovery hints (e.g., suggested replacement model names), causing users to lose actionable guidance and see only a vague "The selected model is not available" message.

The onboarding wizard resolves search providers from a static list without checking plugin enablement rules, allowing users to select and persist a provider that will silently be blocked at runtime, resulting in a non-functional web search configuration.

In auto mode, custom embedding plugins are collected but never correctly returned as a fallback because `createProvider` is called with their IDs despite only supporting built-in providers, causing an unknown-provider error instead of using the discovered plugin.

An unconditional `Math.max(..., endedAt + MIN_REFIRE_GAP_MS)` guard meant to prevent spin-loops on `cron` schedules is incorrectly applied to all schedule types, causing `every`-style jobs to fire later than their configured cadence and making intervals shorter than 2 seconds impossible to achieve.

Renaming the backend ID from `"acpx"` to `"acpx-plugin"` without maintaining a legacy alias or migration path will break any existing persisted ACP configs or session metadata that still reference `"acpx"`, causing `ACP_BACKEND_MISSING` errors until users manually update their state.

The new Cortex shortcut handlers directly mutate `state.tab` instead of calling `state.setTab(...)`, which bypasses tab refresh logic and browser URL/session state synchronization, potentially leaving the UI on a stale route.

The default `coding` profile's `group:memory` allowlist only includes `memory_search` and `memory_get`, so the Memoria plugin's core remember/forget/list tools are silently blocked for all fresh installs unless operators manually override the allowlist.

The `isFeishuServerError` retry predicate only checks for `Error` instances, causing plain-object 5xx errors (e.g., `{ response: { status: 502 } }`) to be misclassified as non-retryable, which bypasses the new backoff logic and surfaces transient media download failures directly to users.

The `maybeAttachGif` function fetches from Giphy without an `AbortSignal` timeout, causing the entire DM response to hang if the request stalls, which can back up polling responsiveness under intermittent network failures.

Replacing the shallow spread `{ ...base }` with `structuredClone(base)` causes `DataCloneError` exceptions for valid but non-serializable JS objects (e.g., `URL`, `Headers`, or functions) passed as cron job input, turning what should be clean validation errors into unhandled handler exceptions.

The setup instructions only tell users to install Playwright's browser binaries via `npx playwright install chromium`, but omit `npm install playwright`, causing the script to immediately crash with a `MODULE_NOT_FOUND` error on fresh machines since the Node module itself is never added to the project.

When updating a nested provider field in JSON/value mode, the helper returns `null` for paths that aren't exactly 3 segments, causing `collectDryRunRefs` to skip dependent refs and allowing `--dry-run` to incorrectly report success even when existing refs would become unresolvable after the provider change.

The `withTimeout` wrapper uses only the global agent default timeout instead of the per-session timeout override, causing premature turn aborts and a behavior regression when a session is configured with a longer timeout than the global default.

Returning `true` for the generic grammY "failed after N attempts" wrapper in `isSafeToRetrySendError` is unsafe because that envelope can mask ambiguous transport failures where Telegram may have already accepted the message, risking duplicate sends.

`SharedStateProcessor.clear()` zeroizes the buffer but never truncates the vector, so the buffer appears to still contain data (as zeroed bytes), which can incorrectly block future `addData()` calls and cause `getData()` to return stale zero-filled data.

The session picker hard-codes "main" as the primary session key instead of reading the configured `mainSessionKey` from `OpenClawChatSessionsDefaults`, which will cause incorrect session switching and status display for any install that sets a custom main session key.

When multiple Signal messages are debounced and batched together, combining all message text but only retaining the most recent `quoteTarget` causes a plain follow-up message to be misattributed as part of a quoted reply, resulting in incorrect `ReplyTo*` context.

Replacing `normalizeCommandBody` with `normalizeCommandBodyLite` in `buildCommandContext` breaks alias canonicalization, causing alias commands like `/id` in group mentions to fall through unhandled instead of being mapped to their canonical equivalents like `/whoami`.

The `isJsonPayload` check incorrectly excludes top-level JSON scalars (strings, numbers, booleans, null) by only matching `{` or `[`, causing valid JSON output from `--json` commands to be routed to stderr instead of stdout, breaking automation that relies on capturing that output.

When a Google search via Bright Data returns a non-JSON HTTP 200 response (e.g., an HTML error page), JSON parse failures are silently swallowed and returned as empty results, causing callers to incorrectly treat a failed request as a legitimate query with zero matches.

When re-onboarding an existing Azure model, the stale per-model `api` field is never cleared or overwritten, causing runtime resolution to continue using the old adapter (e.g., `openai-completions`) even after the provider has been updated to a new value (e.g., `openai-responses`).

When a tool call targets a bare Telegram DM chat ID without a `:topic:` suffix, the new `targetExplicit` early return in `resolveTelegramAutoThreadId` discards the active thread ID, causing replies to fall back to the root DM instead of the correct topic and breaking session continuity.

Setting `connected: false` unconditionally in the `finally` block during Telegram poll retries can cause the gateway to report as fully unready during recoverable outages, because the `disconnected` status is not gracefully ignored by the readiness check the way other transient states are.

MiniMax Zen models (`minimax-m2.5`, `minimax-m2.5-free`) are incorrectly classified as `anthropic-messages` instead of the OpenAI-compatible `chat-completions` endpoint, causing any discovered MiniMax model to be routed to the wrong API endpoint when selected.

By making the allowlist prompt conditional on `quickstartDefaults` and removing the `forceAllowFrom` fallback in `finalize`, callers that set `forceAllowFrom` without quickstart defaults will silently skip the intended DM allowlist enforcement for the Zalouser channel.

The `toolsExpanded` flag is derived solely from `activeSession?.verboseLevel`, which only reflects persisted overrides and not inherited defaults (e.g., `agents.defaults.verboseDefault = "full"`), because `loadChatHistory()` drops the resolved verbose level returned by the backend, causing tool cards to incorrectly render as collapsed for sessions relying on inherited configuration.

Using broad `includes` substring matching for exec completion event strings (e.g., "exec completed", "exec failed", "exec killed") can falsely classify unrelated system events like cron reminders as exec completions, causing `resolveHeartbeatRunPrompt` to misroute or suppress those messages with the wrong prompt handler.

The regex-based source rewrite in `root-alias.cjs` incorrectly mutates non-import content such as template strings and comments that match `import "./x.js"` or `from "./x.js"` patterns, which can silently corrupt runtime string data and should instead be limited to parsed import/export specifier nodes.

When `thinkingLevel` is set to `"off"`, the `think: false` flag is incorrectly nested inside the `options` object rather than being placed at the top level of the Ollama `/api/chat` request body, rendering the thinking-suppression feature ineffective on the native Ollama path.

In `resolveTaskScriptCandidates()`, checking the Startup-folder fallback path before the explicit `OPENCLAW_TASK_SCRIPT` override means a stale startup launcher can silently take precedence over a caller-specified script when scheduled-task query fails, causing probe, stop, or restart operations to target the wrong script on custom or dev installs.

On Windows, `resolveVersionFromPackage()` follows `realpathSync` on `.cmd` shims but never reaches the actual `acpx/package.json`, causing the fallback to return `null` and leaving the original `execution-failed` error unrecovered.

The thinking-to-text conversion always prepends the synthesized thinking block to the front of the message, corrupting the original text block order and causing downstream providers to reconstruct history with a different context sequence than what the model originally produced.

The early return added when `totalTokensFresh === false` causes `resolveTotalTokens()` to discard valid input/output token totals before reaching the existing fallback calculation, resulting in `subagents_list` reporting no token counts at all for runs where only partial usage data is available.

When a fallback model is used, skipping `setSessionRuntimeModel` leaves the session in a mixed state where the persisted `contextTokens` reflect the fallback run but the stored model reflects the primary, causing downstream session math like gateway utilization summaries to be skewed.

When a payload has `mediaUrl` set but `mediaUrls` is an empty array, the nullish coalescing operator (`??`) incorrectly treats the empty array as a valid value, causing `resolvedMediaList` to be empty and silently dropping the media attachment instead of falling back to the single `mediaUrl`.

The session-key error fallback is disabled too early—immediately upon gateway acknowledgment rather than after the relay receives a lifecycle start event—so if the ACP runtime fails on its first lifecycle event, the parent relay can still hang because the real child run ID hasn't been learned yet.

The `sessions_list` tool still serializes only `entry.totalTokens` instead of falling back to `totalTokensEstimate`, so sessions on providers that omit usage data will now expose zero context size to agents even though a preserved estimate exists.

The XML parameter parser fails to convert scalar boolean values like "true" and "false" from string to their proper boolean types, causing tools that rely on strict boolean checks to behave incorrectly—treating string "true" as falsy or string "false" as truthy.

When `--json` mode is active, `restoreLogsToStdout()` is only called on the success path, so if `ensurePluginRegistryLoaded` throws, `loggingState.forceConsoleToStderr` remains permanently enabled, causing all subsequent stdout log output to be incorrectly redirected to stderr for the rest of the process.

The early return for slash-containing model IDs in `buildQualifiedChatModelValue` strips the provider prefix, causing models like `meta/llama-3.3-70b-instruct` to be sent without their provider namespace (e.g., `nvidia/meta/...`), which can route requests to the wrong provider or conflate distinct models with the same slash-containing ID.

The fallback markdown tool-call extractor fails to apply `allowedToolNames` filtering in the additional-pattern loop, allowing arbitrary JSON objects and `<tool_call>` blocks to be incorrectly promoted to tool calls even when no matching configured tool exists.

When `replyToMode` is `first`, Slack channel IDs matched case-insensitively by the auto-thread resolver can populate `threadId` for `[[reply_to_current]]`, but the `hasRepliedRef` flag in the action runtime only flips on an exact-case match, so the first-reply budget is never consumed and subsequent sends in the same turn incorrectly auto-thread again.

Switching to `listEnabledFeishuAccountConfigs()` can incorrectly suppress `feishu_chat` tool registration in multi-account setups where an earlier (alphabetically) account has `tools.chat: false`, because the first-account chat check now sees a non-chat-enabled account that was previously filtered out, hiding the tool even when a later account has chat enabled.

The new video retry heuristic requiring `content` to be passed is never triggered in production because all existing callers of `downloadMessageResourceFeishu` omit the `content` parameter, causing `isVideo` to always be false and iOS video downloads to silently fall back to a single retry instead of the intended three-attempt backoff.

Adding `GRAMMY_NETWORK_REQUEST_FAILED_AFTER_RE` as an allowed retry path in `isSafeToRetrySendError` is unsafe for non-idempotent Telegram sends because grammY's "failed after N attempts" error can wrap ambiguous transport failures where the message may have already been delivered, risking duplicate user-visible messages.

The foreign-owner suppression path uses a stale startup config snapshot (`params.cfg`) instead of the live config, which can cause valid thread messages to be incorrectly dropped after hot config changes in multi-account setups.

The `extractMarkdownImageUrls()` function processes raw text before code blocks and quoted content are handled, causing Markdown image syntax inside fenced code blocks, inline examples, or user-quoted content to be incorrectly treated as real images — stripping them from the output and uploading their URLs, which corrupts documentation or tutorial responses about Markdown syntax.

The updated `stripSpecialMarkupFromText` function fails to remove standalone separator headers (e.g., `#+#+#+#+#` or `#+#+#+#+# assistant to=final`) because only `NO_REPLY` remainders are treated as removable, allowing internal scaffolding markers to leak through to users in normal reply flows.
Using `|| true` to suppress the exit status of `vagrant up` causes `cmd_start` to silently swallow failures and falsely report success, leaving users unaware that the VM never started.

When a provider reports only `promptTokens`, the CLI session update path unconditionally overwrites `inputTokens` and `outputTokens` with `undefined` instead of preserving the previous counters, causing reused CLI and cron sessions to display `?/?` token counts despite the auto-reply path correctly preserving them.

The `SKILL.md` file references required scripts (`evening_analysis.py`, `night_training.py`, `dashboard.py`) that were never added to the repository, meaning any invocation of this skill will immediately fail with missing file errors.

The early `kind: "final"` return for rate-limit errors bypasses the normal `buildReplyPayloads()` deduplication logic, causing a second failure message to be appended to users who already received content via streaming or a messaging tool.

The fallback logic in `applyGigachatNonInteractiveApiKeyChoice()` incorrectly blocks all stored auth profiles, not just legacy Basic auth ones, causing valid stored OAuth credentials to be ignored and forcing users to redundantly re-supply their API key on repeat runs.

The orphan short-reply reattachment logic runs unconditionally for any missing session key, which can silently override an explicit user session switch by reassigning `sessionKey` to a wrong candidate session when the first message is a short token like "yes" or "ok".

When the DeepInfra model discovery endpoint fails, the fallback to `buildStaticCatalog()` persists zero-cost pricing entries to `models.json`, causing `resolveModelCostConfig()` to incorrectly report $0 costs for affected model routes until a successful fetch overwrites the stale data.

The fetch wrapper incorrectly resets the consecutive network failure counter on all 4xx responses, including proxy-generated errors like `407 Proxy Authentication Required` that never reach Telegram, preventing the fast polling-restart path from triggering during the exact proxy outages it was designed to recover from.

Changing `KillMode` in `buildSystemdUnit()` only affects newly written unit files, so existing Linux users will retain the old `KillMode=control-group` setting on disk until they manually reinstall the service, meaning the targeted bug will persist through a normal upgrade and restart.

Calling `runContextEngineMaintenance()` after `contextEngine.compact()` returns means that for legacy and delegated engines, post-compaction side effects like session-memory indexing and `after_compaction` hooks fire against the pre-maintenance transcript, leaving downstream consumers with stale data.

When `wrapStreamFnSanitizeMalformedToolCalls` removes an assistant message entirely, it forwards the rewritten history to the provider without re-running turn validators, potentially transforming the original malformed tool call error into a different provider-side rejection due to consecutive user turns violating Anthropic's alternating-turn requirement.

When re-authenticating with LiteLLM, newly discovered `contextWindow` and `maxTokens` limits are never applied to existing model entries because `applyProviderConfigWithDefaultModel()` skips updates when the model ID is already present, leaving users permanently stuck with stale limits from their initial onboarding.

The regex `\bgot status:\s*internal\b` is too broad and will incorrectly match human-readable HTTP 500 messages like "got status: Internal Server Error", causing those errors to be misclassified as timeout/failover events instead of preserving their original error text.

An unconditional global sort by `attachmentIndex` applied after synthetic placeholders are merged overrides intentional ordering set by `selectAttachments()` and `runCapability()`, breaking configurations like `attachments.prefer: "last"` and disrupting expected capability output sequencing.

Defaulting `intervalMinutes` to `15` instead of `0` silently enables periodic background sync for deployments that had explicitly disabled all other auto-sync triggers, causing unexpected CPU and embedding traffic every 15 minutes unless users discover and manually override the new default.

When a heartbeat is pruned due to no delivery target or an unready channel, `restoreHeartbeatUpdatedAt` is never called, leaving the session's `updatedAt` timestamp bumped and causing idle sessions to appear permanently active, breaking the idle-reset freshness contract.

The optimization bypass logic fails to account for animated PNGs (APNGs), which share the `image/png` MIME type and will still be passed through the image optimization pipeline, causing them to be silently flattened to a single static frame.

The timeline's day-of-month and day-of-week checks are incorrectly combined with AND logic instead of OR logic, causing combined DOM+DOW cron expressions (e.g., `0 9 1 * 1`) to only plot dates that satisfy both conditions simultaneously, while the actual Croner runtime correctly fires on either condition, resulting in missing run markers and incorrect pending/past status labels.

An incorrect index into `closeMatches` causes `extractThinkingFromTaggedStream()` to select the wrong closing `</think>` tag when stray leading close tags are present, leaking raw control-tag text into the reasoning stream instead of properly identifying the last closed `<think>` block.

A fresh 3-step 429 backoff wrapper applied on every attempt within a loop of up to 32 retries can compound into multi-minute stalls under persistent rate limiting, because the backoff budget resets each iteration instead of being bounded across the entire run.

The bundled Codex auth hint guard incorrectly keys off `runtimePlugin.id` (the provider name) instead of `pluginId` (the owning plugin), causing any custom plugin that registers the `openai` provider ID to be mistakenly treated as the bundled OpenAI provider and shown incorrect Codex OAuth credentials.

Moving `ensureRuntimeHandle` outside the `try/catch/finally` block means initialization failures skip session error-state updates and completion metrics, potentially leaving stale session metadata and observability gaps.

The new thread context inheritance fallback ignores `replyToMode` and `hasRepliedRef`, causing messages sent under a `replyToMode="first"` session to incorrectly continue threading all replies instead of only the first one.

The markdown image extraction logic collapses all non-image text into a single `remainingText` block sent only after all images are uploaded, destroying the original interleaved text/image ordering and making it impossible to associate captions with their corresponding images.

The updated `ignored` predicate incorrectly filters out all non-`.md` files, silently preventing multimodal files (e.g., `.png`, `.wav`) in `extraPaths` from ever triggering watcher updates and leaving them perpetually stale in the index when multimodal support is enabled.

The fallback guard only skips `setSessionRuntimeModel` but still persists the `cliSessionId`, so CLI providers will reuse the fallback session on subsequent requests, defeating the intent to retry the primary model after recovery.

The timer-driven post-run path in `onTimer()` still calls `recomputeNextRunsForMaintenance` with the default `consumeReloadRepairSkip: true`, unlike the manual run fix, which can cause a concurrently-edited invalid job's skip marker to be prematurely consumed and its `scheduleErrorCount` incorrectly incremented an extra time, potentially auto-disabling an unrelated job one maintenance cycle too early.

When a provider buffers commentary into `result.payloads` instead of streaming deltas, the streamed hosted-tool branch completes with an empty assistant text item because it doesn't fall back to payload text like the non-stream path does, causing prefatory text such as "Let me check that" to be silently dropped before the `function_call` item is emitted.

The `persistSessionUsageUpdate()` guard incorrectly requires `promptTokens > 0`, so when a provider reports `promptTokens: 0` after a session reset, the function falls through to the stale/clear branch instead of persisting an explicit empty snapshot, causing `/status`, gateway rows, and TUI views to display outdated or `unknown` token counts instead of `0`.

The new `mediaMaxMb` config limit is effectively unreachable for WebSocket callers because base64-encoded attachments expand by ~33%, causing any value above ~18 MiB to exceed the 25 MiB WebSocket frame cap and get dropped before the attachment parsing logic ever runs.

The config parser incorrectly rejects the valid empty array value for `retrieveMemoryTypes` because `parseMemoryTypes` returns `undefined` for `[]`, causing `safeParseMemoriaPluginConfig` to raise a validation error and fail plugin initialization even though an empty array is a legitimate "no filter" input.

`hasWebSearchKey()` hard-codes known web search providers instead of querying the dynamic plugin registry, causing the security audit to falsely report web search as disabled even when a custom third-party provider is active at runtime.

Because `.ci/multipass` is nested inside the root `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, running `pnpm --dir ./.ci/multipass install --frozen-lockfile` resolves the root workspace instead of multipass's own lockfile, leaving multipass without its dependencies and causing subsequent `pnpm dev roundtrip` steps to fail on missing modules.

The code hardcodes `openclaw-gateway.service` as the target systemd unit instead of respecting user-defined profiles or the `OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT` environment variable, causing the companion app to monitor and control the wrong gateway instance in multi-profile setups.

Moving `loadGlossarySources()` after the early return for empty new terms means malformed glossary JSON files and invalid `--lang` arguments will no longer be caught by CI when doc changes contain no new title or link-label terms, allowing broken glossary files to silently pass and fail later in the translation workflow.

The `borderRadius` and `setBorderRadius` props were accidentally removed from the prop bag passed to the Appearance tab, causing the Roundness slider to display `undefined%` and become non-functional, breaking the user's ability to change the UI corner radius.

The newly added `maximum: 50` constraint on the `limit` parameter in `SessionsListToolSchema` conflicts with existing callers that pass `limit: 200`, potentially preventing the tool from enumerating enough sessions to locate older or less-active targets in larger deployments.

If `systemd_init()` fails before `tray_init()` is called, the error state is silently dropped and the tray menu permanently displays "Status: Unknown" instead of the expected "User Systemd Unavailable" message.

The documentation incorrectly marks image-only thread roots as supported for vision pipeline attachment propagation, when in fact the code drops root messages lacking text, meaning attachments from image/PDF-only thread starters will never be passed to subsequent replies.

Filtering out legacy services before rendering cleanup hints causes actionable removal instructions to disappear precisely when automatic cleanup was skipped, leaving users with no guidance on how to manually remove detected legacy gateway services.

The 1-second cap on the metadata grace window in `agent.wait` can cause intermittent loss of `stopReason` and `pendingToolCalls` structured metadata when a run's post-lifecycle work (e.g., session updates) exceeds that timeout before the dedupe terminal snapshot is written.

The new test calls `transcribeOpenAiCompatibleAudio`, which is not imported in the file, causing a `ReferenceError` at runtime that breaks the test suite and leaves the intended filename-extension behavior unverified.

The session history helper silently discards media-only turns (image/audio/file attachments with no text) because `extractTextContent()` returns an empty string for them and the `trim()` check filters them out, causing plugins to skip those turns and pull in stale context when building the recent-history window.

When `agentCfg.reasoningDefault` is set to `"off"`, it is not treated as an explicit override, so `resolveDefaultReasoningLevel()` later upgrades reasoning back to `"on"`, making it impossible to disable default reasoning on reasoning-capable models via configuration.

Although `registerSubagentRun` now stores `requesterMessageId` on the registry entry, the downstream `startSubagentAnnounceCleanupFlow` function never reads and forwards `entry.requesterMessageId` to `runSubagentAnnounceFlow`, causing completion announcements to omit `currentMessageId` and fall back to unthreaded delivery, defeating the reply-binding this change intended to preserve.

The webchat-to-external-channel mirroring logic uses a stale snapshot of `sessionEntry` captured at runner creation time instead of re-reading the current session store, meaning if `lastChannel` or `lastTo` updates mid-flight, the mirror will be sent to the wrong channel.

In `group_topic_sender` Feishu topics, the unfocus resolver checks the shared `chat:topic:root` binding before the sender-scoped one, so `/unfocus` can incorrectly remove the shared ACP binding and report success while leaving the sender's actual focused binding intact.

The `postCreateCommand` in the devcontainer config runs `npm install` instead of `pnpm install`, which in a pnpm workspace will skip all workspace packages and leave the environment in a broken state where build and test commands fail due to missing dependencies.

Making `appPrincipal` required in the Zod schema will break existing valid Google Chat deployments that omit it, even though the `app-url` and `project-number` webhook verification paths don't require it at runtime.

When a legacy engine rejects `sessionKey` in an early method like `bootstrap()`, the fast path in `wrapContextEngineWithSessionKeyCompat` bypasses `invokeWithLegacyCompat()` for subsequent calls like `assemble()`, causing the engine to throw if it also rejects other new fields like `prompt`.

When a gateway service transitions to a stopped or uninstalled state, stale health and probe snapshots are never cleared and new samples are blocked from replacing them, causing the diagnostics window to indefinitely display misleading "healthy" results from the last active run.

The skill's documentation only warns about unsafe code execution during `scan` and `test` commands, but omits a warning that `init --agent` also executes unreviewed third-party code via `spec.loader.exec_module(...)` during module inspection, leaving users vulnerable during the documented setup flow.

The `conversationReview` flag is effectively a no-op in the mixed MMS query path because it is only used to raise the minimum `limit` to 25 in `effectiveSearchParams()` but never causes `querySmsMessages()` or `querySmsMmsMessagesByPhone()` to perform a broader scan, meaning older MMS rows that the feature is intended to surface will still be omitted.

In `announceScriptOutput`, `job.sessionKey` is not passed to `resolveDeliveryTarget` when resolving `delivery.channel="last"` targets, causing script output to be incorrectly routed to the agent's main session instead of the job's intended thread-specific session.

The `reply_to_guid` field is being set in the RPC params, but the current `imsg` helper's `handleSend` RPC handler does not read or process this field, making the reply-threading fix a silent no-op in production.

When syncing a fresh auth profile credential to sibling agent directories, cooldown/disabled state is not cleared for those siblings, causing them to remain blocked from using the newly propagated credential until the cooldown window expires.

The owner allowlist cache is keyed only on the raw array reference and plugin/account/provider IDs, so hot-swapping a plugin with a new `formatAllowFrom` implementation but the same plugin ID will silently reuse stale normalized owners and grant or deny commands incorrectly until the `ownerAllowFrom` array reference changes.

When `session.historyLimit` is set to `0`, switch targets are still built from persisted `sessionHistory` entries, allowing old sessions to remain reachable via `/session` commands instead of being disabled as documented.

The `handleStdout` function ignores the new `detectCursorKeyMode()` helper and instead uses two independent `includes` checks to update `session.cursorKeyMode`, causing the wrong final mode to be set when both `smkx` and `rmkx` sequences appear in the same PTY chunk because the `rmkx` branch always wins regardless of byte order.

Unconditionally clearing `contextLookupProvider` for slash transcript model IDs forces model-only lookup in `resolveContextTokensForModel()`, causing `/session-status` to skip provider-configured context windows and incorrectly fall back to the 32k default for sessions where the context window is defined under a provider and bare model ID combination.

The new `soulFile` config key is written and persisted but never read by the runtime — the agent bootstrap and prompt assembly still hardcode `SOUL.md` — so configuring a custom soul file has no effect on the persona used for replies.

When deriving symlink names in the composite workspace, using raw workspace basenames without reserving internal names like "memory" can cause collisions with OpenClaw's reserved paths, leading the memory backend to incorrectly index linked repositories as memory data and blocking normal memory-folder behavior.

The `normalizeImagePayloadForModel` function fails to re-validate attachment size after converting HEIC images to JPEG, allowing transcoded images that exceed the configured size limit to pass through to the model pipeline.

The new system-scope systemd availability check incorrectly requires a zero exit code from `systemctl status`, causing OpenClaw unit operations to fail on degraded systems with unrelated failed units, even though the user-scope path already correctly accepts non-zero exit codes in this scenario.

The runtime context estimation omits token costs for images loaded via `detectAndLoadPromptImages`, causing the context engine to over-assemble history on multimodal prompts and resulting in avoidable token overflow or compaction.

The `controlUrl` guard only catches Headscale incompatibility when explicitly configured in the gateway config, but fails to detect cases where the local Tailscale daemon is already connected to a custom control server, allowing `mode=serve|funnel` to pass validation and produce an opaque runtime failure instead of the intended fast-fail error.

When `activateGlobalHookRunner` is false, plugin loading still mutates global command and interactive registries because `shouldActivate` remains true, causing command routing to fall out of sync with the active registry and hook runner on cache misses.

The auto-observe hook repeatedly stores the same tail messages on every `agent_end` call without checking if they were already captured in a prior run, causing duplicate memories to accumulate over long conversations and degrading retrieval quality and increasing cost.

The `symlinkPathWithFallback` fallback on non-admin Windows can copy `node_modules` as a real directory into `dist-runtime`, which the cleanup step in `tsdown-build.mjs` won't remove on subsequent rebuilds (since it only deletes symlinks), causing stale dependency trees to persist and degrading build/watch performance over time.

The hotspot updater's default `--lane unit-fast` doesn't match the CI-emitted lane names `unit-fast-1` and `unit-fast-2`, causing it to silently filter out all CI summary lines and write an empty hotspot set unless the caller manually overrides the lane flag.

The Mermaid fence-to-placeholder conversion in `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml()` is applied globally, but since `scheduleMermaidRender()` is only called in the chat lifecycle, views like the markdown sidebar and agent status panel will render a blank box instead of a diagram or code fence.

The new Exa API key credential path (`plugins.entries.exa.config.webSearch.apiKey`) is never registered in the secret target registry, so secret discovery, auditing, and resolution tooling will silently fail to enumerate or manage Exa web-search keys.

Replacing the scoped `pnpm install --prod` with a plain `pnpm install` during `ui:build` bootstrap now triggers a full workspace dev dependency installation, making builds unnecessarily heavy and prone to failure in constrained or offline environments.

The workflow uploads an IPA artifact intended for direct sideloading, but uses an `app-store` export method via `fastlane beta_archive`, which produces an IPA packaged for App Store/TestFlight distribution that cannot be directly installed by end users.

The health-ping logic forces a hard reconnect when a periodic presence update fails, inconsistently treating what the initial connect-time code handles as a non-fatal, best-effort error, which can trigger reconnect loops and unnecessary downtime when presence updates are flaky but the underlying connection is still functional.
The `lastReflectionBySession` map grows unboundedly because entries are never evicted in production, risking memory exhaustion in long-lived gateways with many unique sessions.

Thinking event recipients are registered too late — after `onAgentRunStart` fires — so any reasoning deltas emitted before the first agent event callback (e.g., from `emitReasoningStream()`) are silently dropped for opted-in WebSocket clients.

Applying `escapeXml` to skill names before embedding them in the truncation notice causes skills with special characters (e.g., `&`, `<`, `>`) to be displayed and recommended as their escaped equivalents (e.g., `&`), which then fails the exact-match lookup in `formatSkillInfo()`, making those omitted skills undiscoverable via the advertised `openclaw skills info <name>` command.

Registering Xiaomi as an image-capable provider causes image understanding to fail on default Xiaomi installs because the default onboarding model (`mimo-v2-flash`) is text-only and will be rejected by `describeImageWithModel`.

The deduplication guard only checks the current plugin registry instance, so it fails to prevent duplicate tool registrations when the gateway's deferred-channel flow triggers two separate loader passes that each produce a fresh registry, leaving the original double-registration bug unresolved.

The alias-fallback logic in `filterSkillEntries()` bypasses exact-name policy misses for skills whose names are canonically equivalent (differing only by `_`, `-`, or `.`), causing both skills to pass through and trigger a collision error in `workspace.ts`, making it impossible to selectively enable one of two canonically-colliding skills via `skills.policy` even when distinct `skillKey` values are assigned.

Two non-existent auth plugin entries (`google-gemini-cli-auth` and `minimax-portal-auth`) were manually added to the bundled plugin metadata snapshot without corresponding plugin directories, causing a mismatch between the checked-in snapshot (77 entries) and the auto-generated output (75 entries) that will fail the snapshot validation test in CI.

The shared "feishu" provider-wide bot name alias is registered but never unregistered on stop or when no identity is resolved, causing `resolveBotName(["feishu"])` to return a stale bot name from a previous run for subsequent hook events.

The new `shouldSync` freshness gate skips reading external CLI credentials when the local token isn't near expiry, causing stale or revoked credentials from Codex/Qwen/MiniMax CLIs to go undetected until the token approaches expiration.

The configured-binding registry is primed before `loadOpenClawPlugins` activates the new plugin registry, causing the cached prime result to be immediately stale and deferring compile errors for newly loaded channel adapters to runtime instead of surfacing them at gateway startup.

The automatic reset hook (`session:*_reset`) is emitted after `archiveSessionTranscripts` renames the old transcript file, causing legacy session entries without `previousSessionEntry.sessionFile` to generate empty memory notes because `findPreviousSessionFile` can no longer locate the archived conversation.

The new markdown image extraction path fails to pass `replyInThread` to `sendMediaFeishu()`, causing extracted images to be posted outside the thread instead of as proper thread replies when `sendText()` is called in a Feishu topic/thread context.

The dedupe grace timer incorrectly reuses the full original `timeoutMs` instead of the remaining timeout budget after the first `Promise.race`, allowing `agent.wait` to exceed the caller's requested deadline by up to an additional full timeout window.

When `rawInput === "current"` in a sandboxed (`restrictToSpawned: true`) context, the literal key lookup resolves before alias fallback but ignores `spawnedBy` constraints, so an unrelated session named "current" in the store can match first and block resolution of the requester's own session, causing `sessions_history`/`sessions_send` to silently fail or return incorrect results.

Passing `OPENCLAW_HOME` to services without also preserving `USERPROFILE` causes tilde expansion to fail on Windows, breaking startup and stop/restart flows for services that use the documented `~`-prefixed home directory override.

The package version is being downgraded from `2026.3.23` to `2026.3.22-beta.1`, which would cause a version regression that could break or confuse downstream publish and update workflows if this commit is merged after the higher version has already been released.

`toProviderQualifiedModelKey` incorrectly bails out for slash-containing model IDs instead of combining the separate `provider` and `id` fields into a qualified key, causing provider-specific context window limits to be conflated across providers under the same upstream model ID.

Removing `workflow_dispatch` from the Docker release workflow eliminates the only way to manually retrigger or backfill GHCR image publishing for existing release tags, leaving no recovery path if a Docker publish fails.

The retry wrapper is applied at the entire `sendPayload` level rather than individual Telegram requests, so when a payload fans out into multiple chunked or attachment requests, a mid-delivery failure will cause already-delivered chunks or media to be re-sent, duplicating content on retry.

The Twitch extension now imports from the new `openclaw/plugin-sdk/twitch` subpath, but no minimum `openclaw` peer dependency version is declared, allowing older hosts to install the package and fail at load time when their loader cannot resolve the new subpath alias.

The new `execApprovals` field is added to the TypeScript type for Slack config but is missing from the Zod runtime validation schema, causing it to be silently rejected as an unrecognized key during config parsing and making the feature impossible to enable.

The `toWorkspaceRelativePath()` function incorrectly identifies paths as outside the workspace by checking if the relative path string starts with `..` rather than checking for actual `..` path segments, causing valid filenames beginning with `..` (e.g., `..env`, `..reports/file.txt`) to be rejected even when they resolve within the workspace.

When `stop_scope="step"` is specified, `step_id` is only recommended rather than required, allowing a producer to emit a valid step-scoped STOP without identifying the target step, which creates ambiguity that could cause runtimes to halt the wrong step or the entire chain.

The plugin TTS registry returns `fileExtension` without a leading dot, causing audio files to be saved with malformed names and breaking downstream MIME type inference that expects dotted extensions like `.mp3` or `.opus`.

The regex used to strip leaked function-call preludes is too narrow, only matching hardcoded patterns like `callrecipient{...}`, so variations with arbitrary function identifiers or extra spaces will slip through and get incorrectly persisted as assistant text on tool-use turns.

Malformed `agentId` values in voice-wake route targets (e.g. `"!!!"`) are silently normalized to `"main"` by `normalizeAgentId` instead of being rejected, causing wake-word traffic to be misrouted to the default agent without any validation error.

The schema cap on `limit` was lowered to 1000, but the underlying snapshot backends still support up to 2000 nodes, causing valid large-page snapshot requests to fail schema validation before they ever reach the implementation.

Adding "discord" to VOICE_BUBBLE_CHANNELS only sets the `audioAsVoice` flag on the payload but the Discord outbound adapter ignores that flag for routed/queued sends, causing Discord voice bubbles to silently fall back to normal attachments in all but the immediate-reply code path.

The fallback to `launchctl load -w` incorrectly treats an exit code of 0 as success without inspecting stderr/stdout, which can cause a false success report even when `launchctl` printed a real failure message, since the legacy `load` command is known to return exit code 0 even on meaningful errors.

Placing the secret-detection matrix strategy at the top-level job causes all steps (checkout, setup, and audits) to run 104 times instead of only the intended secret-scanning step, massively inflating CI runtime and flakiness.

After a gateway restart, `buildGatewaySnapshot()` relies on an unpopulated in-memory cache for the latest Cortex capture history, causing the Overview card to incorrectly show Cortex as idle and lose the last-capture timestamp/reason until a new capture occurs, even though the data exists on disk.

The session-saved info log is emitted immediately after the inline write rather than after post-hook actions, meaning it can create a false-positive audit trail and potentially persist sensitive data in logs even when a later hook blocks or redacts the session file.

Tool result content is inserted verbatim inside triple-backtick code fences without escaping, so if a tool's output contains triple backticks (e.g., from markdown or code snippets), the fence closes prematurely and corrupts the exported transcript.

The `autoTopicLabel` setting is looked up only from the top-level `channels.telegram.direct[chatId]` config, bypassing account-scoped overrides and the `"*"` wildcard fallback, so `autoTopicLabel: false` can be silently ignored and unexpectedly trigger costly LLM rename calls on first DM messages.

The `isFromFallback` check incorrectly suppresses `setSessionRuntimeModel` even when hook-driven model overrides (not true fallbacks) occurred, causing the session's persisted runtime model to drift from what actually executed.
