The replay path accepts any non-blank, whitespace-free tool call name without validating it against the current tool schema allowlist, allowing unavailable or unknown tool names to be forwarded to strict provider replayers and potentially causing request rejections, while diverging from the existing sanitization policy enforced elsewhere in the codebase.

In the new markdown-image extraction branch, `sendOutboundText` omits the `replyInThread` flag that `sendMediaFeishu` correctly passes, causing mixed text-and-image responses in threaded Feishu conversations to be split — with images posted inside the thread and remaining prose posted to the root message instead.

Classifying all `browser act kind="click"` actions as navigation boundaries incorrectly resets the search loop streak on harmless clicks, allowing repetitive search-click patterns to evade loop detection entirely.

Excluding `plugins.installs` from the manifest-registry cache key means that within the 1-second TTL, installs or removals of global plugins that collide with bundled plugin IDs won't be reflected, causing the duplicate-suppression logic to use a stale winner/loser set and potentially load the wrong plugin version.

The new `MODULE_NOT_FOUND` fallback in `importResolvedPluginSdkSubpath` silently imports source TypeScript files directly instead of the published `dist` targets, causing the smoke test to pass even when the plugin-sdk's export map is broken for downstream consumers.

The bundle MCP inspection logic still flags any non-`command`-based server as unsupported, so SSE/URL-backed bundles will be incorrectly reported as having an unsupported transport in warnings, status output, and CLI inspection even when they are functioning correctly.
Each HTTP poll passes the original full timeout value instead of the remaining deadline, allowing the total batch operation to significantly exceed the configured maximum wait time.

The `conversationReview` flag is only honored when an explicit `phoneNumber` is provided, but not when a `contactName` search resolves to a single number, causing older MMS rows to be silently omitted in the latter case despite identical intent.

When a fallback model is used, the cron session correctly avoids persisting the fallback model/provider, but still persists `contextTokens` derived from the fallback model, causing inconsistent session metadata where the stored primary model and stored context window size describe different models.

The draft stream is not sealed before returning from preview finalization, allowing stale partial callbacks that arrive asynchronously after dispatch to overwrite the already-finalized preview content when the stream is later stopped.

The `formatTransportErrorCopy()` call is gated inside the `ERROR_PREFIX_RE` block, so bare transport errors like `socket hang up` or `connect ECONNREFUSED` that lack an `Error:` prefix are never rewritten into user-facing copy and pass through unchanged to assistant streams and history replies.

The `memory_refresh` tool truncates memory IDs to 8 characters in search results, but the subsequent replace call requires a full UUID, causing the documented two-step refresh workflow to fail when callers copy the displayed ID into `memoryId`.

Replacing `/Query` with `/Query /FO LIST` in `assertSchtasksAvailable()` can cause false "schtasks unavailable" failures on Windows hosts with large task inventories, because the full listing output may exceed Node's default `execFile` buffer limit.

Defaulting `supportsUsageInStreaming` to `true` based solely on the provider ID is unsafe because vLLM/SGLang providers accept arbitrary base URLs that may point to proxies not implementing `stream_options.include_usage`, risking streaming parse failures or missing responses.

When all batched documents fail to resolve, the fallback path dispatches each failed message once via `processMessage` but then incorrectly enqueues a second caption-only batch turn, causing the agent to receive the same captioned document message twice when batching is enabled.

The documentation incorrectly instructs users to run `openclaw plugins enable google` instead of the actual plugin ID `google-gemini-cli-auth`, which will cause the Gemini OAuth flow to silently fail because the wrong (inert) plugin is enabled.

The retry loop silently resolves ambiguous slash-prefixed model IDs by binding to the first matching provider instead of rejecting the ambiguity, potentially switching auth and capability behavior without any user signal.

`handlePTTCommand` is wired into the command handler but `/ptt` was never registered in the shared commands registry, causing it to be treated as plain text by inbound debouncers, which can result in burst messages being incorrectly merged, and the command remaining absent from slash-command menus.

The Dockerfile hardcodes the AVX2-optimized `bun-linux-x64.zip` build for amd64, causing illegal-instruction crashes on older CPUs that lack AVX2 support, which the previous installer script handled automatically.

The `reply_to_guid` field is incorrectly populated with the SQLite row ID instead of the iMessage GUID because `MessageSid` is sourced from `message.id` rather than the message GUID, causing threaded replies to fail to attach to the correct parent message.

The `trading-agents` skill specifies a `pip` installer that is not supported by OpenClaw's whitelist of accepted install kinds (`brew`, `node`, `go`, `uv`, `download`), causing the install step to be silently dropped and leaving users unable to install the required `tradingagents` dependency through OpenClaw.

Unconditionally applying `tryRecoverLatin1AsUtf8` to all `filename=` header values corrupts legitimately Latin-1-encoded filenames (e.g., `Ã©.txt`) by misinterpreting their bytes as UTF-8, breaking round-trip filename fidelity for downloads.

The `canUseTool` check only validates general tool support but not whether the `image` tool is specifically available, meaning non-vision models may be incorrectly instructed to use an image tool that is absent due to missing configuration or policy filtering, leading to contradictory prompts and failing tool plans.

Adding `bad-barnacle` to the stale exemption list has no effect because PRs are actually auto-closed by a separate workflow (`auto-response.yml`) that triggers on the `dirty` label, meaning the intended protection against Barnacle closures is never applied.

When OpenClaw is launched via a transpiler cache from a different working directory without an explicit `argv1` parameter, `resolveLoaderPluginSdkPackageRoot` fails to fall back to `argv1`-based resolution, causing `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` subpaths to go unresolved and breaking plugins that import modules like `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-runtime`.

Because `loadPluginManifestRegistry()` retains duplicate plugin IDs for both bundled and workspace-overriding plugins, a shadowed bundled web-search plugin (e.g., `google`) is still included in `compatPluginIds`, causing it to be incorrectly auto-allowlisted and preventing the expected "not in allowlist" validation warning from ever triggering.

When `agentId` is omitted, `filterSkillEntries` silently falls back to resolving the main agent's skill policy, which can cause non-main agent sessions to have skills incorrectly shown or hidden based on the wrong agent's policy overrides.

The per-channel `replyToMode` override is never passed to the deferred reply resolver, causing queued followups and other deferred Slack replies in channels with a channel-specific `replyToMode` config to incorrectly fall back to the account-level threading mode instead.

If a user types a draft message while a new session creation RPC is in-flight, the auto-switch logic in `createNewChatSession` will still call `switchChatSession()` upon completion, which clears `chatMessage` and silently discards the draft the user typed while waiting.

The mobile screenshot uses only a narrowed viewport instead of full device emulation (user agent, isMobile flag, touch support), causing sites that detect these signals to render their desktop experience and produce inaccurate mobile screenshots.

Thinking events are only sent to recipients registered at run start, so a control UI that reconnects or joins a session mid-run will never receive thinking updates because there is no late-join or session-scoped fallback subscription path.

The image model override only passes the primary vision model without its dedicated fallback chain, so if the primary image model fails, requests will incorrectly fall back through the text model's fallback list instead of the configured vision-model fallbacks.

The regex used to parse the `description:` field from YAML frontmatter only captures the inline value on the same line, so skills using YAML block scalar syntax (e.g., `description: |`) will have their description reduced to the literal character `"|"`, causing `analyze-trigger` to produce incorrect low-relevance or false-negative scores instead of evaluating against the actual description text.

The typo-rejection heuristic runs before plugin provider lookup, causing valid custom memory providers whose IDs resemble built-ins (e.g., `openaix`, `ollama2`) to be incorrectly rejected at config-validation time instead of being matched against loaded plugins first.

When a model directive like `gpt-4o@20251001` is used without an explicit provider, the numeric-profile fallback resolves against `defaultProvider` instead of the inferred allowlist provider, causing the `@YYYYMMDD` suffix to persist in the model token and breaking fuzzy/allowlist matching for providerless shorthand flows.

During LaunchAgent repair for Bun-runtime installations, the code discards the valid Bun binary path already stored in the plist and attempts to re-resolve it via `which bun` in the npm lifecycle's restricted PATH, which can cause repairs to fail even when the original Bun binary is still accessible.

When a zero-token snapshot arrives for an upgraded session row, the legacy `totalTokens` field is incorrectly treated as current because `totalTokensFresh` is absent, causing the fallback logic to restore the old token total instead of reflecting the fresh zero and making session size displays revert to stale pre-upgrade values.

When a post-`/new` or `/reset` turn fails before `resolvedThinkLevel` is computed, the session entry is written with a fresh ID but retains stale `thinkingLevel` and `verboseLevel` fields, causing the retry to incorrectly inherit the old `/think` or `/verbose` settings instead of starting from defaults.

When `readSubagentOutput` returns a non-empty partial progress banner after a timeout, the `!reply?.trim()` guard short-circuits the retry polling loop, causing the system to announce stale intermediate output instead of waiting to retrieve the actual final assistant message.

In the intentional-override path for installed global vs. bundled duplicate plugins, using `continue` silently drops the bundled candidate from `manifestRegistry.plugins` entirely rather than just suppressing the warning, causing downstream plugin-status lookups in `loadOpenClawPlugins` to incorrectly omit the shadowed bundled plugin as an overridden/disabled entry.

When `usePty` is true on Windows, the `windowStyle` option is lost because the PTY code path returns early with only `ptyCommand` and the supervisor later reconstructs the shell with a plain `getShellConfig()` call, ignoring the user-configured `tools.exec.windowStyle` setting.

The fallback logic incorrectly classifies any non-cron run with a truthy `sandboxSessionKey` (which can be a synthetic or auto-derived identifier) as a `"main"` session target, causing background and tooling runs to be mislabeled and potentially triggering wrong alerts or remediation paths.

The `deepseek-api-key` auth choice is exposed in onboarding, but the DeepSeek plugin will silently fail to apply because `deepseek` is not added to `BUNDLED_ENABLED_BY_DEFAULT`, meaning the provider runtime remains disabled unless the user manually enables the plugin first.

Stripping `---\nQueued #N` markers from arbitrary inbound text can cause pasted or quoted collect-mode prompts containing inline directives (e.g., `/fast`, `/think`) to be incorrectly recognized and applied as real user directives, unintentionally mutating session settings.

The new conjunction matcher uses `.*?` which doesn't cross newlines, causing `hasUnbackedReminderCommitment()` to miss multi-line reminder promises and silently skip the unscheduled reminder warning that users rely on.

The `value.deliver !== false` guard silently swallows hook failures that are returned as `status: "error"` results rather than thrown exceptions, breaking observability for background automations because no main-session system event is injected when `deliver` is false.

The new `withCheckedContinuation` wrappers in `requestEventAccess` and `requestWriteOnlyEventAccess` do not observe Swift task cancellation, so timed-out calendar permission requests will remain suspended until the user responds to the OS prompt, potentially accumulating stuck tasks and degrading node responsiveness in headless sessions.

The new `onToolStart` kickoff path skips the `shouldSendToolSummaries` gate, causing group chats and native-command flows to incorrectly receive tool kickoff status messages that should be suppressed.

When `sendMediaFeishu()` fails, the already-stripped markdown image URLs are silently dropped from the message instead of being preserved as plain-text links in the fallback output, causing content loss in error scenarios.

The new `shouldPreferNativeJiti` branch replaces `getJiti().import()` with a raw dynamic `import()` for certain helper paths, which will cause `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` failures for Matrix plugins that ship source-style `.js` wrapper files relying on Jiti's module resolution fallback to handle unbuilt `.ts` sibling references.

Within the same `modelCallback` handler, `buildModelsProviderData` is called with the reloaded `runtimeCfg` while other operations like `formatModelsAvailableHeader`, `resolveStorePath`, and `resolveDefaultModelForAgent` still use the stale startup `cfg`, causing a config snapshot mismatch that can display updated model options in the UI but persist the selection to the wrong store or compare against an outdated default model.

After a tab-close action, the fallback logic in `getPageForTargetId()` returns the surviving tab's URL instead of the closed tab's URL, causing the enriched `url` field in close responses to be incorrect and potentially misattributing audit or policy logs to the wrong page.

Setting `overflow: clip` on the usage card container clips absolutely-positioned dropdown menus used by filters and export controls, causing options that extend beyond the card bounds to be cut off and potentially unreachable, which is a functional regression in the usage filtering and export workflow.

The prepaint theme script reads the first matching `localStorage` key rather than the active gateway's scoped key, which can apply the wrong theme when multiple gateways have saved settings, causing a visible flash until the app corrects it on hydration.

HTTP 529 errors are inconsistently classified as "rate_limit" for object-shaped errors but "timeout" for string-shaped errors, causing Anthropic overload failures to be bucketed differently depending solely on the error wrapper format.

Bumping the Kotlin compiler plugin versions to 2.3.20 without also upgrading the AGP-bundled Kotlin/KGP (via a `buildscript` override) causes a version skew that can break Android sync and compilation.

When `totalTokensFresh` is false and the provider emits no usage, `resolveTotalTokens()` silently falls back to stale `inputTokens`/`outputTokens` left over from a previous run, causing token counts in `/subagents list` and completion stats to be incorrectly reported as belonging to the current turn.

The post-compaction AGENTS.md context refresh (up to 3000 chars) is injected after the compactor has already calculated its budget, meaning the added content is excluded from the compaction sizing and can cause smaller-context models to immediately overflow again, wasting a recovery attempt.

The model-switch guard resolves context window token limits using only the model ID, ignoring the target provider, causing false rejections when switching to a provider with a larger context window that shares the same model ID as a smaller-windowed provider.

The `--json` flag is falsely advertised as CI/CD-friendly machine-readable output because the CLI unconditionally prints human-readable preamble lines (e.g., "Using spec", "Adapter: ...") to stdout before the JSON block, making the combined output unparseable by tools like `jq`.

In `simple-chunker.ts`, `splitEntryIfNeeded` checks byte size to decide when to split but delegates to `splitLongLine` which enforces character length instead, causing CJK/emoji-heavy lines to exceed the configured byte budget and produce oversized chunks in multilingual content.

In `buildStatusMessage()`, the token-count overwrite logic can replace a newer `totalTokensEstimate` (written during compaction) with an older snapshot from `readUsageFromSessionLog()`, causing `session_status` to report a stale pre-compaction prompt size instead of the current stored estimate.

When `gateway restart` is run after a stop on macOS, the fallback unconditionally returns `null` on any `service.restart()` failure, swallowing actionable launchd bootstrap errors (e.g., GUI-session guidance) and causing headless/SSH/wrong-user invocations to be misdiagnosed as a missing service installation.

The `count` parameter is accepted and used as part of the cache key but is never sent to the SearXNG backend or used to truncate results client-side, causing all requests to return the same default number of results regardless of what the caller specified.

Adding `DEFAULT_SHIELD_FILENAME` to `VALID_BOOTSTRAP_NAMES` causes nested `SHIELD.md` files in monorepos to be injected into the agent prompt, even though shield policy enforcement only reads the root-level `SHIELD.md`, misleadingly presenting security guidance that has no actual effect on the tool set.

The browser search loop detector stores history under the pre-navigation tab ID instead of the updated `targetId` returned after a `browser navigate` call, causing subsequent same-tab search actions to be keyed to a stale ID and making `findRecentBrowserTargetContext()` unable to retrieve prior search context, which prevents the `browser_search_storm` detector from catching follow-up in-tab query refinement loops.

Setting PAT auth token expiry to effectively infinite causes `setTimeout` to overflow Node.js's maximum delay of 2,147,483,647 ms, resulting in the refresh timer firing nearly immediately and continuously, creating unnecessary CPU usage, log churn, and repeated auth work.

The `download` function for native Feishu documents exports a PDF but provides no reliable way for the agent to read its contents, since the read tool lacks PDF support and the dedicated PDF tool is optional, leaving exported documents effectively inaccessible on default installations.

When the session store entry contains a stale zero token count, the new fallback ordering causes `buildGatewaySessionRow()` to emit `0` instead of the fresher transcript usage total, causing gateway session listings and TUI consumers to display incorrect live context figures.

When a bundled plugin is explicitly enabled in config, its provider IDs are returned by both the bundled catalog path and the plugin hooks path, causing duplicate model entries to appear in model listings and picker flows.

The mermaid copy button uses an inline `onclick` handler that will be silently blocked by the app's Content Security Policy, which explicitly disallows `'unsafe-inline'` scripts, rendering the copy button completely non-functional in production.

When resolving a provider-qualified alias (e.g., `vercel-ai-gateway/sonnet`), the code returns the alias's target model ID verbatim without re-normalizing it through `parseModelRef`, causing the constructed provider/model key to skip required normalization steps and potentially produce an invalid or unrecognized model ID for providers like Vercel AI Gateway that expect a compound path format.

The configured `webSearch.timeoutSeconds` value is never read, causing `runBrightDataSearch()` to always fall back to a hard-coded 30-second timeout and making the operator-facing config setting effectively non-functional.

The `applyMediaUnderstandingIfNeeded()` call uses pre-override `originalProvider/originalModel` values instead of the final resolved model, causing unnecessary media transcription and increased token usage for users who have explicitly selected a vision-capable model via channel overrides or saved session settings.

The `durationMs` field in the new usage lifecycle event is sourced from `result.meta.durationMs`, which only reflects the final successful model attempt, so runs that exhaust one or more fallback models will report an artificially short duration compared to the true end-to-end elapsed time captured in `startedAt`.

The change to allow `tokensAfter >= 0` lets a legitimate zero token count from zero-usage backends be persisted as `totalTokens = 0` with `totalTokensFresh = true`, causing the session to appear empty after compaction instead of falling back to the previously known token size.

The new regex for Z.ai error code 1311 is added to `ERROR_PATTERNS.billing`, but `isBillingErrorMessage` skips that pattern for messages longer than 512 characters due to an early length-gate, causing billing errors wrapped in longer payloads to be misclassified (e.g., as rate limit errors) downstream.

The `skills list` fallback recovery path is insufficient because it only surfaces a skill's name but not its `SKILL.md` file path, leaving truncated skills permanently unusable since the model cannot provide a `<location>` to `read`.

The `extensionFastInfraChanged` predicate omits the shared `vitest.scoped-config.ts` file from its hard-coded watch list, meaning changes to that shared config will not trigger the `extension-fast` CI lane, potentially allowing breaking changes to silently skip testing across all extension lanes.

The `hookTimeoutMs` field was added to the Zod runtime schema but the generated base config schema (`GENERATED_BASE_CONFIG_SCHEMA`) was not regenerated, meaning config-schema consumers like the gateway API and editor UIs will be unaware of the new option despite runtime validation accepting it.

The `phase: "usage"` event incorrectly reports only the duration of the final successful model attempt rather than the total end-to-end latency across all fallback retries, causing latency undercounting in dashboards for any auto-reply run that triggers the fallback path.

When isolated mode is enabled, `OPENCLAW_HOME_VOLUME` is written directly into Docker Compose volume name fields without validation, so a bind-style path like `/srv/openclaw-home` will be treated as a volume name and cause setup to fail after the costly image build/pull step.

New Copilot reasoning models are registered in `resolveDynamicModel` but omitted from the shared model catalog, causing them to incorrectly default to thinking `off` instead of the expected low-thinking default, which silently degrades behavior for users who switch to those models.

The troubleshooting documentation incorrectly directs users to configure `llm.providers` to resolve vision errors, but the attachment-analysis resolver actually reads from `cfg.agents?.defaults?.imageModel`, meaning users who follow this guidance will update the wrong key and still be unable to use Slack attachment analysis.

When a zero-usage run updates a session with a new model or provider but unset legacy totals fields, the code incorrectly copies the previous model's `totalTokens` into `totalTokensEstimate`, causing inaccurate context size displays and potentially misguiding the `parentForkMaxTokens` guard under the new model's identity.

The new `sms.search` command is registered and advertised when READ_SMS permission is granted, but it lacks a corresponding entry in the live-test command profiles, causing live Android-node capability tests to abort with an "unmapped advertised commands: sms.search" error on devices with that permission.

Loop hook payloads omit `runId` and `attemptIndex`, so when `runEmbeddedAttempt` retries after compaction, auth refresh, or truncation, handlers cannot distinguish a fresh attempt's `iteration: 1` from the original, causing per-turn telemetry to be ambiguous or double-counted.

Fatal gateway errors (e.g., 4014 disallowed intents) emitted during the startup readiness wait window are captured by the early guard but never re-drained before `waitForDiscordGatewayStop` registers its error handler, causing the error to be silently swallowed and reported as a generic timeout or reconnect failure instead of an actionable message.

The fallback to `Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone` for auto-detecting the cron timezone can return `undefined` in certain Node.js environments, silently creating the same implicit-timezone job the change intended to prevent, so a hardcoded fallback (e.g., `"UTC"`) is needed before treating the timezone as resolved.

The `extractAssistantText` helper in `session-export.ts` only handles assistant message content as an array of typed blocks, so legacy sessions where `msg.content` is a plain string will export as empty assistant turns, silently dropping conversation text from older transcripts.

The documentation instructs skill authors to register custom slash commands and session hooks (e.g., `/careful`, `/freeze`) that the skill runtime does not actually support, meaning those commands will never be invocable.

Because PTY stdout is delivered in arbitrary chunks, checking each chunk independently for `\x1b[?1h`/`\x1b[?1l` sequences can miss split sequences or ignore a later `rmkx` in the same chunk, leaving `session.cursorKeyMode` stale and causing `process send-keys` to emit the wrong arrow-key encoding.

Changing the condition from `> 0` to `>= 0` in `derivePromptTokens` causes it to incorrectly return `0` instead of `undefined` when no prompt-side token breakdown is provided, leading to session storage and status displays falsely reporting an empty context for backends that only emit a total token count.

The `coerceApngSniffToPng` function incorrectly overwrites the `image/apng` MIME type with `image/png` when the file extension or header suggests PNG, destroying the only signal downstream callers have to distinguish animated PNGs from static ones, which causes APNG stickers to be uploaded with the wrong content type.

The documentation incorrectly groups Twitch and Nostr under a transport mode where outbound replies are sent over HTTPS, when in reality both channels send replies over WebSocket connections, which could mislead operators into configuring incorrect firewall or proxy egress rules.

In summary-first compaction sessions, legacy assistant messages without timestamps bypass both the timestamp-based and index-based stale usage checks, causing pre-compaction usage to incorrectly persist and inflate the post-compaction context counter.

The sessions_list tool serializer omits the `totalTokensFresh` flag, preventing callers from distinguishing reliable token counts from stale fallback estimates and potentially causing agents to make routing or cleanup decisions based on outdated context sizes.

The new `exa` provider is not added to the config schema's allowed provider enum, so users cannot explicitly configure it and must rely on fragile auto-detection, breaking explicit provider selection.

When block streaming completes, the fallback to raw `payloadArray` instead of sanitized `replyPayloads` means OTEL telemetry can capture control tokens and unsanitized directive text that was never actually shown to the user.

Using `||` instead of `??` causes a legitimate zero token count to be treated as falsy and incorrectly replaced by a stale `totalTokensEstimate`, meaning just-reset or empty sessions will display outdated token totals in `/status`.

Removing the `isBusy || props.sending` guard from the Stop button's render condition means the button can appear and remain clickable while disconnected, even though `handleAbortChat()` silently no-ops without a live connection, leaving users with a non-functional abort control and no access to send or new-session actions until the socket reconnects.

The `onDelivered`/`hookContext` fix for `message_sent` emission is opt-in only, leaving several extension reply paths (WhatsApp, MS Teams, BlueBubbles) still bypassing `deliver.ts` without the new hooks, so issue #47472 remains unresolved for those channels.

Memory-heavy unit files are not excluded from the timed-heavy selection pool, so tests that qualify as both duration-heavy and memory-heavy will be scheduled in multiple lanes simultaneously, defeating the isolation goal and risking OOM pressure in CI.

Adding `src/config/doc-baseline.test.ts` to the `unit.isolated` bucket when it already exists in `singletonIsolated` causes the expensive test file to be executed twice concurrently, regressing runtime and reintroducing the memory spike the patch intended to fix.

Deleting `talk-config-contract.json` causes `talk-config.contract.test.ts` to crash with an ENOENT error at module load time before any assertions run, completely eliminating talk-config contract test coverage.

Passing `envelopeOptions` into inbound metadata formatting causes `Conversation info.timestamp` to be unintentionally suppressed when users set `envelopeTimestamp: "off"`, a setting meant only to control outbound message envelope headers, not inbound conversation timing context in prompts.

The `requestBrowserApiJson()` wrapper never passes a timeout to `withTrustedWebToolsEndpoint`, so if Bright Data's control-plane API stalls, `brightdata_browser_*` calls can block indefinitely instead of failing within the configured timeout.

Switching `queueBodyBase` from `bodyWithEvents` to `effectiveBaseBody` drops prepended system events (e.g., model-switch, node-connected notices) from deferred/queued follow-up prompts in followup/collect/steer modes, causing queued turns to run with less context than immediate turns.

The `prewarmSessionUsageCache` function performs significant synchronous work before its first `await`, meaning that even though it's called fire-and-forget, it still blocks the startup call stack and can delay gateway readiness when many sessions exist.

The `announceTimeoutMs` budget is incorrectly derived from the caller-supplied `timeoutSeconds` rather than using an independent, larger window, meaning that short-timeout callers will still have late replies dropped because the follow-up `agent.wait` and announce steps are constrained by the same tight deadline they were trying to escape.

When a Slack thread starts with a file-only message and media hydration fails, the thread context is left with no body or media, causing replies that reference the parent file to reach the agent with no indication a file ever existed.

A new basename guard in `resolveEntrypointFromPackageJson` causes renamed `.cmd` shims (e.g., `qmd-beta.cmd`) to return early before attempting the adjacent `package.json` lookup, silently breaking Windows plugin resolution when `resolveCliSpawnInvocation` is called with `allowShellFallback: false`.

The documentation incorrectly promises automatic next-day log recovery for all configurations, when in reality users with a fixed `logging.file` path will experience indefinite write suppression after hitting the file size cap since no daily rotation occurs to reset it.

When no MIME type is provided, `normalizeImageDataUrl` incorrectly defaults to `image/png`, which can cause non-PNG images (e.g., JPEG, WebP) from other clients to be mislabeled and fail to render after a page reload.

When a session is started with `background: true`, `process send-keys` may immediately fall back to normal-mode CSI escape sequences before the PTY application has emitted its `smkx` startup sequence, causing arrow and navigation keys to be misinterpreted in the exact launch-then-interact workflow this change intends to fix.

The streaming-usage guard checks the entire config file for `supportsUsageInStreaming` instead of scoping the check to Bailian models specifically, which can cause the patch step to be incorrectly skipped, leaving other Bailian models without the required compatibility flag.

The new `totalTokensEstimate` field is not cleared during session resets or compaction, causing stale token counts from previous conversations to persist and be exposed in `openclaw sessions --json` output, which can mislead automation consuming that data.
The Baidu plugin's version (2026.3.16) is mismatched with the root package version (2026.3.14), which will cause the release validation script to fail and block the release process.

The `brightdata_browser_network_requests` tool never clears `session.requests` after click- or submit-driven navigations, causing subsequent network history dumps to include stale traffic from the previous page.

The root-help fast path uses `emitCliBanner` to prime the script tagline cache, but since `emitCliBanner` exits early in non-TTY environments, piped or CI help output will silently render an empty tagline instead of the expected text.

The `persistTestSessionStorePath` function incorrectly preserves the first non-empty `session.store` value it finds instead of overwriting it with the new `storePath`, causing stale session store paths to persist across multiple seeds or suite-scoped tests for any gateway code reading directly from config rather than `testState.sessionConfig`.

The Azure-specific CLI options (API key, base URL, model ID) are not forwarded when `applyAuthChoice` is called during interactive onboarding in `setup.ts`, causing them to be silently ignored and re-prompted for, making these flags only functional in `--non-interactive` mode.

The `sessions_spawn` notification API lacks an `notifyAccountId` field, making it impossible to specify which account should send the completion notification, causing unreliable delivery in multi-account setups where the target chat belongs to a different account than the requester.

`pendingToolResults` is always initialized to `0` instead of being seeded from existing session history, causing the counter to undercount tool results already present at the tail of history when a retried attempt resumes after tool-result truncation recovery, which breaks retry telemetry and token accounting for plugins on the first model turn.

The refactored catch block in `loadInternalHooks` discards `err.message` in favor of only `err.code`, silently swallowing actionable error details (e.g., syntax errors or failed imports) and collapsing them to `UNKNOWN`, which significantly hampers debugging.

The AIMLAPI "missing/invalid key" guidance is incorrectly triggered by a broad auth error matcher that also catches unrelated failures like model deprecation or subscription expiry, causing users to be misdirected to key setup instead of receiving proper fallback handling.

The fallback logic in `resolveDeliveryAttempted` incorrectly returns `true` for any requested delivery, even when execution fails before a delivery attempt occurs, leading to inaccurate `lastDeliveryAttempted` telemetry.

The recovery notice incorrectly assumes the recovered read returned all content from the offset to EOF, but if the underlying tool truncates the result, the message will mislead the agent about how much content was actually returned.

The new `/root` path guard in `setup.sh` will abort the documented quick-start for users running the script in a root shell, even though `/root/.openclaw` paths are valid and explicitly documented in `docs/install/hetzner.md` as long as ownership is corrected with `chown -R 1000:1000`.

The newly added `reasoningDefault` config field is never surfaced in `session_status` output because `buildStatusMessage()` isn't passed a `resolvedReasoning` value and the status fallback path still hardcodes `reasoningLevel ?? "off"`, causing the status display to incorrectly report reasoning as off even when `reasoningDefault` is set to `"on"` or `"stream"`.

When timeout-triggered compaction fails (returns `compacted: false`), the code skips the existing oversized tool-result truncation recovery logic, meaning sessions dominated by large tool results may still spiral into repeated timeouts or unnecessary rotations even though truncation could have resolved the issue.

When the setup wizard runs within the Gateway/Control UI, `listWebSearchProviders` incorrectly resolves providers from the already-active plugin registry instead of the newly updated `nextConfig` snapshot, causing a provider just enabled by the wizard to appear unavailable and skipping key detection until after a restart.

The `allowKeep: authChoice !== "ollama"` change causes `promptDefaultModel` to rebuild the model picker from an unordered catalog, discarding the remote-mode cloud default computed by `promptAndConfigureOllama` and potentially steering Ollama remote-mode users to the wrong default model.

When a user-scope systemd action fails and falls back to the system scope, if the system-scope action also fails, the original user-scope error is thrown instead of the system-scope error, hiding the true cause of the failure from the caller.

The new `DEFAULT_FALLBACK_STATUS_CODES` constant omits HTTP 529 (overloaded), so callers using `fallbackOnErrors: "default"` will silently fail on the first model instead of continuing the fallback chain when an Anthropic/Together-style overload response is received.

Changing the availability probe to `/Query /FO LIST` causes it to buffer the full verbose task listing, which can exceed Node's default `execFile` `maxBuffer` on hosts with many scheduled tasks, incorrectly making `schtasks` appear unavailable.

Because `runSweep()` is only triggered by `setInterval` and never called immediately after the startup grace period expires, pending deliveries from a crash or restart will be delayed until the first interval tick (up to 2 minutes) rather than the intended 30-second grace window, undermining the post-restart recovery guarantee.

The CalVer "already up-to-date" fix is applied to `resolveUpdateAvailability()` but not to `formatUpdateOneLiner()`, so users will still see a false update prompt even though `availability.available` is correctly set to false.

The new `preserveWebp` path uses the default 6 MB image cap instead of Discord's larger upload cap, causing valid 6–8 MB WebP files to fail in Discord interaction-reply and component-send flows that set `preserveWebp: true` without supplying `maxBytes`.

The issue creation command never passes `--label` to `gh issue create`, so issues filed through this skill will be missing the canonical `bug`/`enhancement` labels defined in the templates, breaking triage and searchability.

Because `scheduleChatScroll()` is queued before `scheduleMermaidRender()` and completes within ~150ms while Mermaid rendering takes significantly longer (due to lazy import and SVG generation), the chat scroll position is calculated before the Mermaid diagram expands the page, leaving bottom-pinned users with the transcript incorrectly scrolled away from the latest message.

Calling `resolveBootstrapContextForRun()` solely to determine reset prompt wording causes bootstrap hooks to execute twice per run, doubling any latency or side effects from arbitrary hook logic before the real run invokes them again during Project Context construction.

When a provider only reports `total_tokens` without an `input`/`output` breakdown, the code incorrectly preserves stale `inputTokens` and `outputTokens` from the previous session entry instead of clearing them to unknown, causing misleading token split data to appear in `/status` and session listings.

When a user previously configured GigaChat using Basic auth via `models.providers.gigachat.apiKey`/`baseUrl` without a `gigachat:*` auth profile, the OAuth onboarding flow fails to reset the old custom Basic URL, causing subsequent OAuth requests to be incorrectly routed to the Basic endpoint until the user manually edits `models.providers.gigachat.baseUrl`.

A timeout created for each lifecycle-first `agent.wait` call is never cancelled when the `dedupePromise` resolves first, causing orphaned timers to accumulate under concurrent waits and leading to unnecessary memory and callback churn.

The documentation for multi-agent orchestration patterns is incomplete because it only instructs users to enable `tools.agentToAgent` without also requiring `tools.sessions.visibility` to be set to `"all"`, which is a prerequisite checked first in `sessions-access.ts` for cross-agent session calls to succeed.

When a session's model is patched, the stale `totalTokensEstimate` is not cleared alongside `contextTokens`, causing the new token-count fallback logic to surface the old model's token usage against the new model's context window in session listings until fresh usage is written.

The accumulated token/cost usage summed across all cron turns is incorrectly attributed solely to the final turn's model and provider, meaning multi-turn or multi-model runs will produce inaccurate cost reporting in downstream systems.

Removing the `plugin.origin !== "bundled"` guard causes `resolveNonBundledProviderPluginIds()` to incorrectly return bundled providers, bypassing the zero-load fast path and forcing heavyweight bundled provider plugins to be imported in test environments where they should be disabled by default, regressing unit and gateway test stability.

When a union schema contains any string variant, the early return on `hasStringVariant` skips boolean coercion for mixed `boolean | enum-string` unions, causing user-entered `true`/`false` text values to remain as strings and fail schema validation instead of being correctly coerced to booleans.

The TUI footer fallback to `totalTokensEstimate` is ineffective because the session state updater in `tui-session-actions.ts` never copies `totalTokensEstimate` from the entry, so the field remains undefined and the footer continues to display incorrect token counts on zero-usage turns.

The `coerceApngSniffToPng` function incorrectly downgrades genuine animated PNGs to `image/png` based on file extension or server header alone, causing real APNGs to be misclassified as static PNGs and breaking downstream callers that depend on the `image/apng` MIME type for animated content handling.

The `.chat-send-btn` style change pairs a dark gray background (`--muted-strong`) with a dark foreground (`--text-strong`) in light mode, dropping icon contrast to ~2.9:1 and making the send button icon difficult to read.

The mention-fallback sanitization only strips single-backtick code spans, so bot mention tokens inside multi-backtick code blocks (e.g., ` ```<@botId>``` `) survive into `textForMentionCheck` and incorrectly trigger a bot response when `mentionedUsers` is empty.

When a PR modifies both infrastructure files (e.g., `vitest.channels.config.ts`) and extension files simultaneously, the `changed-extensions` reason causes the CI matrix to only run extension-specific tests, leaving the modified infrastructure config completely untested.

The OpenAI parameter fallback logic triggers a second request with the legacy `max_tokens` parameter on any non-401 4xx error rather than only when `max_completion_tokens` is explicitly rejected, risking overwriting the true root cause with a misleading `unsupported_parameter` error and making onboarding failures harder to diagnose.

Because the skill's frontmatter lacks an `os` allowlist, OpenClaw surfaces it on Windows despite the example using POSIX-only shell constructs (`set -o pipefail`, `grep`) that will fail in PowerShell/CMD, breaking the advertised JSON workflow on stock Windows hosts.

When coercing a direct `dispatcherPolicy` into env-proxy mode, `proxyTls` options are not mirrored from the caller's transport hints, causing proxied HTTPS paths (e.g., Telegram media downloads) to lose IPv4/Happy-Eyeballs fallback behavior in proxy-enabled deployments.

Adding `gateway.handshakeTimeoutMs` to `FIELD_HELP` without a corresponding entry in `FIELD_LABELS` breaks the required parity between the two maps, causing the UI label to be undefined and the schema quality parity test to fail.

When `sessions.usage.timeZone` is unavailable on older gateways, the catch block redundantly fires a second `usage.cost` RPC instead of reusing the already-successful result from the initial `Promise.all`, causing the expensive cost scan to run twice on every fallback to `utcOffset`.

When block streaming and final-mode TTS are both enabled, the `kind === "block"` guard causes the synthesized audio reply to fall back to the channel root instead of reusing the established thread, splitting a single response across two locations.

The markdown image extractor only skips fenced code blocks but not inline-code spans, so image-like patterns inside backtick-wrapped text (e.g., `![alt](https://...)`) will be incorrectly uploaded as real images and stripped from the message, corrupting documentation or tutorial content.

The Qmemory plugin setup snippet is missing a required `openclaw gateway restart` command, meaning users following the instructions on a live gateway will silently end up with the plugin unactivated until a manual restart is performed.

When a `gateway probe` completes stale after a restart or unit switch, `health_run_deep_probe()` clears the in-flight lock but silently drops the queued eager replacement, leaving Diagnostics stuck displaying the previous probe result until the 60-second timer or a manual refresh.

The `model.usage` event is emitted before `deliverAgentCommandResult()` resolves the actual delivery channel, causing the OTEL telemetry to incorrectly attribute the run to `openclaw.channel=unknown` even when it was successfully delivered over a real channel.

The new transport architecture table incorrectly classifies Twitch as using an outbound WebSocket connection when it actually uses IRC/TCP, contradicting the repo's own Twitch documentation, which could cause users to misconfigure firewall rules or debug via the wrong network path.

When a provider sends a legitimate zero-token context snapshot, `deriveSessionTotalTokens()` returns `undefined`, causing the code to fall back to the previous run's stale token estimate instead of correctly reflecting the reset session as empty.

The updated error handler blindly casts exceptions to `NodeJS.ErrnoException` and logs only `err.code`, causing syntax errors and runtime failures during hook import to collapse to "(UNKNOWN)" and lose the original error message that operators need to diagnose broken hooks.

The documentation contradicts itself by setting `SOUL.md` immutable via `chattr +i` in one section while implying token-alone overrides are sufficient in another, creating a broken emergency path where writes will silently fail unless operators first manually remove the immutability flag.

The `think: false` flag is incorrectly nested inside `payload.options` instead of being set at the top level of the request body, causing Ollama's chat API to ignore it and continue returning unwanted reasoning-only chunks even when `thinkingLevel` is set to `"off"`.

When a Matrix account goes offline, its bindings are dropped from memory but persist on disk, so if a child session is deleted while the account is offline, the `subagent_ended` cleanup never finds and removes the stale binding, causing it to be reloaded from `thread-bindings.json` on the next restart.

The documentation incorrectly instructs provider authors to use an `openclaw.providers` field in `package.json` that doesn't exist in the actual `OpenClawPackageManifest` schema, which could cause providers to silently fail to register since OpenClaw reads provider ownership from `openclaw.plugin.json` and runtime `api.registerProvider()` calls instead.

The `hasHelpFlag` function incorrectly identifies `-h`/`--help` as a help intent even when these strings appear as option values rather than flags, which can cause false positives that trigger unnecessary script resolution and leak output in piped workflows.

Returning `0` from `derivePromptTokens()` is ineffective for error paths because `buildErrorAgentMeta()` drops falsy zero values during its spread, causing downstream session writers to retain stale prompt token estimates instead of clearing them.

When a pre-hook command's output exceeds the 64KB `maxBuffer` limit, Node.js throws an error that causes the hook to be reported as a failure even if the command itself exited successfully, meaning a verbose but otherwise healthy hook can incorrectly block execution based on log volume alone.

When `tools.web.search.kimi.baseUrl` is unset, the code unconditionally inherits `config.models.providers.moonshot.baseUrl`, which may point to a custom proxy that doesn't support Moonshot's native `$web_search` behavior, potentially breaking web search for setups that previously relied on the default Moonshot endpoint.

The new `/model status` branch in the TUI short-circuits before reaching the gateway's full implementation, causing TUI users to see only a potentially stale or undefined `modelProvider/model` string instead of the detailed auth/profile/endpoint view produced by the real handler in `directive-handling.model.ts`.

When `lastCallUsage.total` is missing, the fallback incorrectly computes context size as `input + output`, dropping `cacheRead`/`cacheWrite` tokens and distorting context-window telemetry in the `model.usage` event instead of falling back to the existing prompt-token snapshot.

The documented `--threshold` flag doesn't exist in the CLI and the pass threshold is incorrectly stated as 70 when the tool hard-codes passing grades at 75+, which will mislead users into misreading failing scores as passing and attempting to use a flag that will be rejected.

The unconditional call to `telegramDeps.loadConfig()` on every command invocation means a transient config file error will cause all bot commands to throw and drop replies instead of gracefully falling back to the valid startup configuration snapshot.

The `formatReasoningStatus` switch statement has no default/fallback case, so any unrecognized `reasoningLevel` value stored as an unconstrained string in session data will return `undefined` and render as "Reasoning: undefined" in the status output.

The `chattr +i` immutability command in the hardening steps is Linux-specific and unavailable on macOS, meaning macOS users who follow the instructions will silently fail to apply the protection while believing their system is secured.

The status message builder incorrectly uses stale persisted runtime fields (`entry.modelProvider`/`entry.model`) to determine the current provider and model selection, which will cause `/status` and `session_status` to display outdated model information after configuration changes until a new message is processed.

The `resolveTelegramAllowFromEntries` call in the setup wizard surface is missing the `proxy` and `network` transport parameters, so `@username` resolution during the allowlist setup step will fail for accounts that rely on custom Telegram proxy or network configurations, even though other paths like send/probe/audit correctly use those transport overrides.

The `describeXiaomiVideo` function incorrectly uses the text provider's base URL (e.g., `https://api.xiaomimimo.com/anthropic`) for video requests, causing them to be routed to `/anthropic/chat/completions` instead of the correct MiMo video endpoint under `/v1`.

The `totalTokensEstimate` field was added to only one of the two `sessions.changed` broadcast payloads, leaving the second lifecycle-event broadcast (used by subagent create/status events) still omitting the field, which causes WebSocket/SSE clients to continue receiving null token counts until they perform a full refetch.

The `handleCompactCommand()` wrapper does not pass `sourceProvider` when calling `compactEmbeddedPiSession()`, so manual `/compact` invocations will expose `sourceProvider: undefined` to before/after compaction hooks in relayed or cross-channel sessions, causing trust-aware plugins to incorrectly classify manual compactions differently from normal agent turns.

The draft's CONFIRM payload examples are incomplete — one is truncated and another omits required fields (`confirm_id` and `timestamp`) — meaning developers copying the "minimal JSON" examples will produce malformed confirms that can't be correlated to receipts or used to anchor TTL start times.

When a node appears as paired in `node.list` but is missing from `node.pair.list`, it falls through the cracks of both `filteredPaired` and `filteredConnected` arrays and is silently dropped from `--json` output, even though it is still rendered correctly in text mode.

When a 429 response is received during file download, the `retry_after` value from Telegram's API is lost because `fetchRemoteMedia` flattens the response into a plain string error, causing retries to fire after a generic backoff that may be shorter than Telegram requires, resulting in repeated failed attachment downloads.

The `rewriteLocalSourceJsSpecifiers` function uses broad regex-based text replacement to rewrite `.js` import specifiers, which risks incorrectly modifying non-specifier content like string literals, comments, or template snippets that happen to match import-like patterns, and should instead operate on parsed import/export AST nodes.

The contract test derives both its expected values and actual outputs from the same `resolvePluginProviders` mock, meaning if providers are silently dropped by that resolver, both sides shrink equally and the test falsely passes, defeating its purpose of catching wizard coverage regressions.

The new DeepInfra provider was not added to the hard-coded `envProbeProviders` array, causing `openclaw models list --status` to incorrectly show DeepInfra as unauthenticated even when a valid `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` environment variable is set.

When `includeContent` is enabled but a provider returns no usage data, the OTEL instrumentation silently skips emitting both usage and prompt/completion content spans, causing content tracing to fail invisibly for backends that don't report token counts.

The `totalTokensEstimate` fallback added to the TUI footer will never actually display because the estimate field is never stored in `GatewaySessionList` or copied by `applySessionInfo()`, leaving the footer stuck showing "unknown/..." after zero-usage runs.

The `pollStallThresholdMs` config option cannot be honored for values below 30 seconds because the watchdog timer that checks for stalls still wakes on a hardcoded 30,000 ms interval, making any configured threshold shorter than 30 seconds effectively impossible to trigger in time.

When only one of `notifyChannel` or `notifyTarget` is provided, the subagent spawns successfully but silently skips the completion notification, leaving the caller with no ping and no error to indicate the misconfiguration.

When `editDiscordComponentMessage` fails for an approved decision, the new code silently returns with no user-visible confirmation, whereas the previous flow always attempted a follow-up or reply fallback to ensure the user received feedback.

When a CLI-primary agent (e.g. `claude-cli`/`codex-cli`) configures a non-CLI compaction model override, the soft-threshold memory flush path short-circuits early on `!isCli` before ever reaching `resolveCompactionModelFallbackOptions`, rendering the compaction model override ineffective.

The `documentDebounceLane !== "forward"` guard incorrectly excludes all forwarded documents from the new batching buffer, so forwarded PDFs and other documents without `media_group_id` are never grouped and still arrive as multiple separate turns instead of a single combined one.

The `extractRelevantSnippet` function uses a naive whitespace-split tokenizer that misaligns with the actual search tokenizer, causing snippet anchoring to point at wrong locations for conversational queries and silently failing for short or non-Latin search terms.

The function returns an ambiguous raw model value prematurely when it encounters the first provider conflict, instead of continuing to scan the full catalog for a later entry that matches the preferred provider.

Removing the `next.totalTokens = undefined` line causes stale token counts to persist when a provider omits a fresh snapshot, leading status and context-report surfaces to display outdated token totals instead of showing `unknown` or the correct updated value.

The `isMissingAgentWaitStructuredMetadata` function incorrectly treats any successful snapshot with an undefined `stopReason` as incomplete, causing `agent.wait` to unnecessarily enter a 1-second grace delay even when metadata is already final, introducing avoidable latency for valid runs.

The fix to skip `renderCollapsedToolCards()` when images are present doesn't actually make tool-result images visible by default, because they still end up hidden inside a collapsed `<details>` block further in the rendering pipeline, and the new tests pass only by checking DOM presence rather than visibility.

A synchronous `sendMessageMattermost()` call in `flushPendingPatch()` has no timeout guard, so a stalled or hung Mattermost API response can block final reply delivery indefinitely when a fast model response completes before the 200ms streaming timer fires.

When one SMS permission is already granted and the other is newly granted, `smsPermissionGranted` fails to trigger a state transition (remaining `true` throughout), so the SMS settings row can remain stuck showing the stale "Grant" UI until an unrelated recomposition occurs.

Caching `CLLocationManager.locationServicesEnabled()` at initialization instead of calling it live on each snapshot introduces a regression where the published location availability can become stale if system-wide Location Services are toggled without triggering an app-level authorization change.

After a successful restart re-bootstrap via the `onNotLoaded` fallback path, the service's `loaded` flag is never updated to `true`, causing `gateway restart --json` to incorrectly report `service.loaded: false` and misleading any automation that relies on the JSON status output.

When sending an album with inline buttons and a URL caption where `linkPreview` is disabled, the new carrier `sendTelegramText()` call fails to forward `params.linkPreview`, causing Telegram to unexpectedly display a link preview that the caller explicitly requested to suppress.

Passing `durationMs` unconditionally to `formatResponseUsageLine()` causes the stopwatch indicator to appear on all usage footer modes, including the leaner `/usage tokens` mode where it shouldn't appear — the parameter needs to be gated behind a `responseUsageMode === "full"` check.

Replacing the `<label>` wrapper with a `<div>` for the raw config textarea removes its accessible name association, breaking screen reader and keyboard navigation support for assistive-technology users.

The regression test fixture uses `tool_use_id` for embedded tool result blocks instead of the `toolUseId` field shape used throughout the rest of the codebase, meaning the test may not accurately reflect the production data shape and could fail to catch the real bug it was written to prevent.

The retry callback closes over the original `effectiveForce` value instead of re-evaluating follow state, so a forced scroll can override a manual upward scroll made within the ~150ms retry window and snap the viewport back unexpectedly.

The `tags` parameter schema uses a nested `anyOf` construct that can cause the `openbrain_capture` tool to fail registration on strict providers, since the repo's tool-schema helpers don't normalize nested unions and some providers reject `anyOf`/`oneOf`/`allOf` keywords anywhere in a tool definition.

The `soundchain_platform_docs` tool fetches from `https://soundchain.io/skill.md` without an `AbortSignal` timeout, meaning a stalled connection can cause the tool call to hang indefinitely and block the invoking turn.

When both `tool_choice` and `toolChoice` exist in the merged `extraParams` object, `resolveAliasedParamValue` always favors the snake_case key regardless of source precedence, allowing a lower-priority default to override a higher-priority agent-specific setting.

The `shouldApplyOpenAIAttributionHeaders` function only checks for an explicit `api.openai.com` base URL, missing the case where `model.baseUrl` is unset but the runtime already defaults to native OpenAI, causing valid OpenAI requests to silently skip the `originator`/`User-Agent` attribution headers.

The multipass-openclaw-bridge script fabricates both the sent message and the ACK response locally rather than communicating with a real backend, so end-to-end roundtrip failures in payload mapping, routing, or credentials will never be caught by this test lane.

When all documents in a batch resolve to `null` (e.g., due to Telegram's 20MB limit or exhausted retries), the new early-return logic silently drops the entire batch instead of forwarding placeholder turns to the agent as the original per-message path did.

The `extractImages()` function only searches top-level message blocks for images, so images nested inside `toolResult` content arrays are never extracted and will silently fail to render in the chat UI.

The tests validate a locally re-declared copy of the empty-state logic rather than importing and exercising the real production code, so regressions in the actual `chat.ts` implementation would not be caught by this test suite.

When `OPENCLAW_CONTAINER` is set, host-only service manager hints (e.g., `openclaw gateway install`, `systemctl --user ...`) are still appended to the output, misleading operators in container deployments to follow inapplicable remediation steps instead of simply restarting the container.

When a `preHook.command` trims to empty during cron job creation, the normalizer sets `preHook` to `null`, but the gateway's create (`cron.add`) schema only accepts `preHook` as an object, causing the request to be rejected — meaning the "clear on empty" logic works for patches but silently breaks create operations.

The skill file documents non-existent commands (`operon-guard test <path> --quick` and `operon-guard report <path>`) that don't match the upstream 0.2.0 supported commands (`test`, `scan`, and `init`), which will mislead users into running invalid commands.

When a user re-enters the chat tab, stale `chatAutoScrollBlockId`/`chatAutoScrollMode` state is not cleared alongside `chatHasAutoScrolled`, causing the forced scroll to reuse the old "clamp" mode and land at the block start instead of the true bottom of the latest output.

The fallback error handler incorrectly displays a generic "API rate limit reached" message for all matching errors, even when the failure is specifically a 529 overload error, misleading users with wrong diagnosis and recovery guidance despite distinct overload copy already existing in the codebase.

Replacing dynamic bundled web search plugin ID resolution with a hard-coded static list causes valid configs to fail validation when a statically listed plugin ID is injected into the allowlist but isn't actually available or discoverable in the current environment, resulting in spurious "plugin not found" errors.

The `mentionedJids` values passed to the inbound reply helper's `contextInfo` are not normalized through `toWhatsappJid()` like the outbound sender does, so non-standard formats like `+15551234567` or `whatsapp:+15551234567` will be treated as literal strings instead of real @mentions.

The `resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens()` call cannot correctly suppress stale token counts because the `session` object is constructed without the `totalTokensFresh` metadata, causing `/context` commands to display misleading cached token values after output-only turns or model fallbacks.

Routing `anthropic-vertex` through `normalizeAnthropicModelId()` incorrectly maps unsupported 4.5 model aliases (e.g., `sonnet-4.5`, `opus-4.5`) to valid-looking model IDs that will silently pass parsing but fail at runtime, since the Vertex provider catalog only exposes 4.6 models.

Switching to `splitMarkdownIRPreserveWhitespace` can cause `coalesceWhitespaceOnlyMarkdownIRChunks` to drop a trailing whitespace-only chunk when the previous chunk is at the size limit, reintroducing the very whitespace loss the change was intended to fix.

The `includeAltMemoryFile` flag is evaluated only once at startup, so if `MEMORY.md` is later renamed or deleted, the fallback `memory.md` collection is never registered for the process lifetime, causing the file to stop being indexed until a restart.

The `.chat-bubble-actions` container is only revealed on hover, so keyboard users who tab into the copy/expand buttons will find them invisibly focused since `opacity: 0` is never overridden on `:focus-visible`, creating an accessibility regression.

When a run reports zero tokens after a previously active session, the code incorrectly overwrites the new zero value with the old token estimate, causing stale usage data to persist in session listings and status displays until a future positive-token run occurs.

The session store is unnecessarily written on every agent run even when no messages were sent and no dedupe state changed, causing avoidable write lock contention and `updatedAt` timestamp churn on the reply hot path.

Using `replaceAll` with a string replacement for `@_user_N` placeholders in merged-forward messages corrupts display names containing `$` characters due to special substitution pattern handling, and should instead use a callback-based replacement like `normalizeMentions()` already does elsewhere in the plugin.

The new `skills.priority` config option is not factored into the cached skills snapshot version, so changing priority mid-session will have no effect until a skill file changes or the session is recreated, making the setting appear broken for ongoing sessions and cron runs.

The `standardFontDataUrl` is resolved using `import.meta.url` relative to the source file location, causing the path to incorrectly point to `src/node_modules/...` instead of the repo root when running from source, which will break font loading for image-only PDFs in dev workflows.

The `api.registerHttpRoute(...)` handler example incorrectly returns a `{ ok, body }` object instead of writing to `res` and returning `true`, meaning developers who copy this code will end up with a health endpoint that either fails type-checking or silently never sends an HTTP response.

The TUI fallback to `entry.totalTokensEstimate` will never display estimated token counts because the upstream status payload producer in `status.summary.ts` never populates `totalTokensEstimate` in the session summary objects, leaving context usage rendering as `?` for zero-usage and output-only cases.

Exporting `./plugin-sdk/telegram` and `./plugin-sdk/telegram-core` as package subpaths exposes Telegram-private implementation details as public API, creating an unintended long-term compatibility obligation that contradicts the repo's own test suite, which explicitly excludes these paths from the supported public subpath list.

The `resetChatScroll()` call triggers unconditionally on any `chatStream` transition from `null` to a string, including queued sends and external delta events, which causes users who have intentionally scrolled up to be unexpectedly yanked back to the bottom when a new reply starts.

When coercing a boolean `true` for `web.reconnect.jitter`, the schema hard-codes `0.1` instead of the intended default of `0.25` defined in `DEFAULT_RECONNECT_POLICY`, silently reducing jitter and increasing reconnect synchronization for deployments that use `true` as an enable flag.

When a reply contains inline `<think>` tags with text on both sides (e.g., `Okay.<think>…</think>Done`), splitting them into separate `text` blocks causes `extractAssistantText()` to rejoin them with `"\n"` instead of preserving the original inline sentence, adding spurious line breaks to user-visible responses.

The channel-level `replyToMode` override is applied in `prepare.ts` but not propagated to `buildSlackThreadingToolContext`, causing tool replies to still be sent in-thread instead of the channel root when a channel overrides an account-level `replyToMode: "all"` setting with `"off"`.

The header context display was updated to use `totalTokens`, but the `/usage` slash command still calculates usage from `inputTokens`, leaving two visible usage surfaces in WebChat reporting inconsistent token counts for the same session.

Adding "plugin"/"plugins" entries to the Chinese glossary will force mandatory translation to "插件" across the docs, directly violating the repo's documented requirement in AGENTS.md to keep those terms in English.

Calling `loadConfig()` inside `getHandshakeTimeoutMs()` on every new WebSocket connection before authentication triggers repeated synchronous disk reads and JSON5 parsing whenever connections are spaced more than 200ms apart, causing unnecessary CPU/disk overhead that can delay handshakes.

When cloning a plugin entry with object spread after a null-returning lazy getter, the spread re-evaluates `entry.plugin` and can re-trigger the same `ReferenceError` from a circular-import/TDZ state, causing the graceful degradation to fail.

The `channel-media-adapter` heuristic is too narrowly scoped to `extensions/*` and a short basename list, causing shared core outbound media files like `src/channels/plugins/outbound/direct-text-media.ts` to be excluded from the seam inventory, which leads to underreported media seam coverage in `seamTestInventory`.

Applying the 93-way matrix at the job level causes all steps — including `detect-private-key`, zizmor, and `pnpm-audit-prod` — to run redundantly across every shard, massively increasing CI time and runner usage without any additional coverage, when only the secret scanning step actually needs sharding.

Both local and remote probe-auth secret inputs are always resolved eagerly regardless of the active gateway mode, meaning a broken or stale secret provider on the unused credential branch can unnecessarily abort the `status --all` command.

Replacing the responsive `max-width: min(900px, calc(100% - 60px))` with a hard-coded `max-width: 50%` on `.chat-group-messages` forces message bubbles into a half-width column at all screen sizes, causing excessive text wrapping and poor readability on small/mobile screens.

The service worker fails to exclude `/plugins/**` routes from its cache-first interception logic, causing plugin assets to potentially serve stale content across plugin updates since only `/api` and `/rpc` are currently bypassed.

When `requireMentionInThreads: false` allows the first unmentioned thread reply through, `effectiveWasMentioned` is incorrectly left as `false`, causing downstream directive gating to silently strip inline exec/elevated directives for that turn until the bot receives implicit mentions from thread participation.

Removing `overflow-x: auto` from `.data-table-container` will cause the sessions table's rightmost columns and action controls to be clipped and unreachable on smaller viewports, since the parent layout already hides horizontal overflow.

Adding `fallbackOnErrors` to the shared `AgentModelConfig` type falsely implies the option is supported for image, PDF, and image generation models, when in reality those code paths ignore the field and silently fall back to the old retry behavior.

The `handled` field in `PluginHookBeforeMessageProcessEvent` is typed as the literal `true` instead of `boolean`, preventing plugin authors from returning `{ handled: false }` without type errors or casts, despite the runtime and documentation explicitly supporting `false` as a valid decline value.

On the legacy engine, post-compaction AGENTS.md context is injected twice — once directly into the overflow retry and again via a queued system event drained on the next user turn — causing ~3k characters of duplicate prompt bloat after every compaction.

The Querit search result mapper silently drops the `page_age` field instead of mapping it to the standard `published` field, causing recency signals to be lost when using Querit on time-sensitive queries.

`shouldUseWebSearchProviderSnapshotCache()` ignores `OPENCLAW_PLUGIN_DISCOVERY_CACHE_MS=0`, so the web search provider snapshot cache remains active even when discovery caching is explicitly disabled, causing stale provider lists to be returned when providers are added or removed mid-process.

Removing the normalization of `sessionEntry.thinkingLevel` means that a persisted `xhigh` value is no longer downgraded to `high` before `/compact` runs, causing an unnecessary failed API call and retry when compacting on a model that doesn't support `xhigh`.

The documentation incorrectly implies all Discord and Slack Socket Mode traffic flows over a single WebSocket connection, when in reality outbound messages still use HTTPS APIs, which could lead operators to misconfigure firewall or proxy rules and misdiagnose send failures.

When a user skips search setup, the code still mutates tool policy and fetch settings based on the existing provider value in the config, silently altering search and fetch behavior despite the user's explicit intent to skip.

The hard-coded thinking level labels in `thinking-utils.ts` are not gated by provider or model, so users are incorrectly shown `xhigh` as a valid option on models that reject it and shown unsupported granular levels on Z.AI models that only support a binary off/on toggle.
The relay cleanup timer in `cross-bot-relay.ts` is not `unref()`'d, causing the Node.js process to stay alive for up to 120 seconds after message delivery due to the live event loop handle.

The browser snippet in Step 2 only sets `img.loading = "eager"` to handle native lazy loading but fails to mirror the `data-src`-to-`src` assignment already present in the standalone script, leaving images that rely on `data-src` (common in Astro/React components) blank in the recommended browser-tool workflow.

The `shouldUseProviderWizardCache()` function does not check `OPENCLAW_PLUGIN_DISCOVERY_CACHE_MS=0`, so setting that variable to disable the discovery cache in `discovery.ts` is silently ignored by the provider wizard, causing stale provider snapshots to be reused within the same process despite the user's intent to disable caching.

The tray icon name `openclaw-icon` is hardcoded in the AppIndicator setup, but no corresponding icon asset is installed by the build system, meaning the tray entry will render with a missing or default icon on fresh installations.

Instead of recomputing `promptTokens` by summing raw `usage.input + cacheRead + cacheWrite`, the code should use `agentMeta.promptTokens` so that OTEL prompt-token spans reflect the accurate final-call prompt size rather than incorrectly accumulated totals across multiple API calls in tool-use or compaction runs.

The `/cortex mode show` command incorrectly resolves only the channel-scoped override instead of using `resolveAgentCortexModeStatus()`, causing it to falsely report no override even when a session-scoped override is actively in effect.

The updated anchor fragment `#agent-sandbox` in `sandboxing.md` points to a heading that doesn't exist in `docs/install/docker.md`, leaving Docker users with a broken link that fails to navigate them to the correct bootstrap steps.

Adding `SignalAccountConfig` to the Signal runtime-api export without updating the corresponding allowlist in `runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts` will cause CI test failures even though the build passes.

The Tlon channel is incorrectly documented as plain "Outbound HTTP" in the connection architecture table, when it actually relies on a long-lived Server-Sent Events (SSE) subscription that requires SSE-friendly streaming egress, which could lead to misconfigured proxies or incorrect idle-timeout settings.

The new ContentClaw plugin entry in the community docs omits the required `npm install -g contentclaw` prerequisite step, which will cause first-time users to encounter immediate tool handler failures and mistakenly believe the plugin is broken after installation.

The documentation falsely claims that Slack PDF uploads are processed through vision analysis, when the actual code explicitly excludes PDFs from image classification and prevents extracted PDF images from being forwarded to the model.

The restart deferral logic incorrectly uses a queue depth counter that includes ACP session lifecycle calls (initialize, status, close) rather than only active turn executions, causing config-triggered restarts to be unnecessarily delayed by unrelated control-plane requests while misleadingly logging them as "ACP turn(s)".

The updated anchor link in the Anthropic 429 runbook incorrectly points to the unrelated "quick-start-and-first-run-setup" FAQ section instead of the correct entry addressing HTTP 429 RateLimitErrors, leaving users unable to find relevant troubleshooting guidance.

The DebugDrawer component does not initialize its log state with existing buffered entries on mount, causing the panel to appear empty when opened after events have already been logged until a new event arrives.

Sorting reset transcript files lexicographically by full filename instead of by timestamp suffix can cause an older transcript to be incorrectly restored during session recovery when multiple session IDs are present in the sessions directory.

The `formatReasoningStatus` function's fallthrough default silently maps any unknown or legacy `reasoningLevel` value (e.g. `"low"`) to `"stream"`, causing `/status` to incorrectly report active streaming reasoning instead of surfacing or normalizing the unrecognized value.

The README setup command uses an incorrect CLI syntax (`openclaw channel add synology-chat`) instead of the valid form with the plural subcommand and named flag (`openclaw channels add --channel synology-chat`), causing users who copy-paste it to immediately hit a CLI error before reaching the setup flow.

The `preHook` field was added to the add/update input schemas but omitted from the exported `CronJobSchema`, creating an inconsistency where gateway operations can persist and return `preHook` but consumers using `CronJobSchema` cannot represent or validate it.

The LINE runtime barrel in `extensions/line/runtime-api.ts` uses a relative import that resolves outside its extension package to `src/plugin-sdk/line-core.js`, violating the rule that extensions must only depend on public SDK seams, making it fragile to any repo restructuring.

The Mattermost row in the new connection architecture table incorrectly depicts the channel as outbound-only, omitting the inbound HTTP callback mode required when native slash commands are enabled, which could mislead operators into misconfiguring their firewall rules or reverse proxy setup.

The new `denyCommands` whitespace validation rejects entries with harmless leading/trailing spaces, breaking existing configs that the runtime already handles correctly via `trim()`, and should trim entries before validating instead of failing outright.
