Removing the `next.totalTokens = undefined` line allows stale token counts to persist when only `totalTokensFresh` is set to false, causing subagent UX surfaces that read `totalTokens` directly without checking freshness to display outdated context usage instead of showing it as unknown.

The new `emitCliBanner` priming call in `runCli` is ineffective for the most common `--help` path because `src/entry.ts` short-circuits root help before `runCli` is ever reached, leaving the script tagline still rendering as empty in practice.

Setting `replyInThread` solely from `Boolean(threadId)` ignores explicit `replyToId` values, causing messages that should be inline replies to a specific message to instead be incorrectly re-threaded as topic replies.

A new Tavily extension was added under `extensions/tavily/` but the corresponding auto-labeling rule was not added to `.github/labeler.yml`, breaking PR triage and violating the repo's `AGENTS.md` contribution requirements.

Removing `defaultModel` from Ollama's auth result causes the `--set-default` flag in `openclaw models auth login` to silently fail, leaving the user's default model unchanged instead of updating it to Ollama's recommended default as advertised.

When a `<minimax:tool_call>` block contains both a valid and a malformed `<invoke>` element, the malformed sibling is incorrectly preserved as a plain text block after wrapper removal, causing raw `<invoke>` markup to be surfaced to users instead of being stripped.

The `normalizeSlackText` function aggressively collapses all intra-line whitespace and trims each line, which corrupts meaningful formatting in Block Kit content such as code snippets, indentation-sensitive configs, and markdown tables.
The new `QQbot` plugin entry was appended after `WeChat` rather than inserted before it, breaking the required alphabetical ordering of the community plugins list.

Changing `stdio` from `"pipe"` to `["ignore", "ignore", "ignore"]` sets `proc.stderr` to `null`, swallowing Chrome's diagnostic output and turning actionable startup errors into opaque failure messages that are difficult to debug.

A UTF-8 BOM character (`\uFEFF`) was accidentally prepended to `docs/plugins/community.md`, causing frontmatter parsers that rely on a literal `---` prefix check to fail to recognize the file's frontmatter, which breaks documentation listing and i18n metadata processing for that page.

The updated test now depends on a prebuilt `dist/plugin-sdk` directory instead of building its own output, causing it to fail on clean checkouts or in CI environments where `pnpm build` has not been run before `pnpm test`.

The early return in the document batch handling branch bypasses `inboundDebouncer.enqueue`, causing forwarded bursts containing both text and documents to split into two separate agent turns instead of being coalesced, because the text flushes after 80ms while documents wait up to 1.5s in the `documentBatchBuffer`.

When `blockSessionSave` is pre-set to `true` with a `sessionSaveRedirectPath`, the handler unnecessarily reads the full target file into memory for a rollback snapshot that can never be used, wasting I/O and spiking memory on large files.

The compaction completion notice is unconditionally injected as a chat message even on channels that already surface compaction status via callback-driven reactions, resulting in duplicate UX (both a reaction flow and an extra chat message) instead of being gated behind the same callback check used by the start path.

In queued followup turns, auto-compaction incorrectly sends a hardcoded "🧹 Compacting context..." chat message instead of calling the existing `opts.onCompactionStart`/`opts.onCompactionEnd` callbacks, causing inconsistent behavior on Discord and Telegram where normal turns use platform-specific reaction controllers instead of raw chat messages.

When `preserveWebp: true` is set, WebP buffers returned by `loadWebMedia` are wrapped in an untyped `Blob` with a fallback generic filename, causing Discord to receive them as `application/octet-stream` attachments instead of inline images when no filename or extension is available in the URL.

The separator-removal logic fails to strip the `---` and `**User Message:**` markers in threaded or event-prefixed turns because `hasLeadingMetadataWithSep` remains false when `_sep` is encountered after prepended context has already set `seenUserContent` to true, leaving separator artifacts in sanitized history.

Gating separator removal solely on `didStripMetadata` is insufficient because legacy messages lacking an injected `---\n**User Message:**` divider may have user content that legitimately begins with those lines, causing valid content to be silently stripped after an upgrade.

The `imageSanitization` config is only applied to `screenshot` actions but not to labeled `snapshot` actions in `executeSnapshotAction`, causing `agents.defaults.imageMaxDimensionPx` to be silently ignored for users relying on labeled snapshots for visual references.

A new ERNIE extension was added without a corresponding entry in `.github/labeler.yml`, meaning PRs touching the ERNIE extension won't be automatically labeled, breaking triage and reporting automation coverage for that surface.

The ERNIE provider guide's verification step incorrectly uses the channel-only `openclaw message send` CLI command, which requires channel/target options and does not invoke the ERNIE model, meaning readers following the guide will encounter a usage error or send a channel message instead of testing the provider.

The ERNIE provider documentation shows incorrect token limits (`contextWindow: 128000`, `maxTokens: 65536`) that don't match the runtime catalog values (`119000` and `64000`), meaning users who copy the config example could trigger context or max-token failures due to overbudgeted requests.

Importing `bundled-web-search-registry` causes all bundled web-search plugin modules to be eagerly loaded at module initialization time, meaning `listBundledWebSearchPluginIds()` will fail at load time if any single plugin module is unavailable, breaking callers like config validation before the local `try/catch` can intercept the error.

Stale plugin metadata entries for `google-gemini-cli-auth` and `minimax-portal-auth` were manually added to the generated file without corresponding extension manifests, causing the bundled metadata to fall out of sync with its generator script and breaking the snapshot consistency test.

When re-enabling Cortex without specifying `--mode` or `--max-chars`, the updater silently overwrites previously configured values with hardcoded defaults (`technical` and `1500`) instead of preserving the existing settings.

The `runCortexPreview()` function ignores agent-level `cortex.mode` and `maxChars` settings in favor of CLI defaults, causing the preview to show a different context slice than what is actually injected into replies.

Running `openclaw memory cortex enable` unconditionally overwrites existing `cortex.mode` and `cortex.maxChars` settings with hardcoded defaults, silently overriding any custom configuration when the user only intends to re-enable the feature.

Scoping the `fast` command to `"text"`-only removes it from native command surfaces (Discord, Telegram, Slack), causing it to disappear from slash-command UIs on those platforms even though the handler and documentation still reference it as a native command.

The watchdog LaunchAgent example only forwards `PATH` and omits `OPENCLAW_PROFILE` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, causing health checks and gateway restarts to target the wrong service in non-default-profile setups.

Adding `tempHandle.sync()` before reading file stats can cause otherwise-successful writes to fail hard on filesystems where `fsync` is unsupported or unreliable, even though the sync is unnecessary for the subsequent `dev`/`ino` identity checks that consume the returned stats.

The `memory_forget` function coerces a missing `reason` to an empty string instead of leaving it undefined, which can cause delete requests to fail on APIs that treat `reason` as optional-but-non-empty.

Setting `html: false` in markdown-it disables raw HTML token handling, so pasted HTML snippets bypass the custom escape logic and may be incorrectly linkified or markdown-formatted instead of displayed as literal text, regressing the raw-HTML display fix from #13937.

The new `list-style: none` CSS rules for task list items are scoped only to `.chat-text` and `.sidebar-markdown`, omitting the reasoning block context, so task list items rendered inside reasoning blocks will incorrectly display both a browser bullet marker and a checkbox simultaneously.

The custom `www.` autolink matcher uses an ASCII-only character class (`[a-z0-9_-]`), causing a regression where internationalized domain names (e.g., `www.münchen.de`) are no longer converted to links, unlike the previous marked-based renderer.

The custom `www` autolink rule uses an overly broad boundary check that linkifies text like `"www.example.com"` or `[www.example.com]` in quoted or bracketed contexts, violating GFM autolink extension boundaries and producing false-positive links in logs and JSON payloads.

The `!overrides.<provider>.model` guard prevents a later `model=` directive from overriding an earlier `*_model` directive set in a previous block, breaking the expected last-directive-wins behavior and silently ignoring later user-specified model overrides.

The newly added `(no tz)` warning suffix in `formatSchedule()` can be silently truncated away by `printCronList()`'s 32-character `CRON_SCHEDULE_PAD` limit on longer cron expressions, defeating the purpose of surfacing implicit-timezone schedules to users.

`resolveKnownContextThresholds` ignores the fallback provider from `props.sessions?.defaults`, so sessions with unqualified model IDs and no explicit `session.modelProvider` silently fail to match provider-scoped entries in `KNOWN_CONTEXT_THRESHOLDS`, causing pricing-threshold hints and model-ceiling metadata to disappear.

The guide instructs users to access WebChat at `http://localhost:18789` on the host, but because the gateway defaults to `loopback` binding, VirtualBox/Vagrant port forwarding won't reach the guest's `127.0.0.1`, meaning WebChat will silently fail until the docs also instruct users to switch to a `lan`/`custom` bind or set up an SSH tunnel.

The fallback static catalog incorrectly marks the DeepInfra GPT-OSS 120B model as image-capable, allowing image inputs to bypass local validation gates and reach the provider only to fail, inconsistent with how other catalogs treat the same text-only model.
Adding the "DeepInfra" provider link without a corresponding entry in `docs/.i18n/glossary.zh-CN.json` will cause the `pnpm check:docs` CI step to fail due to missing i18n glossary coverage.

The new DeepInfra extension is missing a corresponding entry in `.github/labeler.yml`, which means future pull requests touching DeepInfra files will not receive the expected auto-applied triage label, breaking the maintainer routing workflow.

The `discoverDeepInfraModels()` function makes unauthenticated requests to the DeepInfra model catalog endpoint, so private or account-scoped models are never discovered even though the provider already resolves a valid API key that could be forwarded.

The code review replaces the generic `normalizeBaseUrl` call with a Gemini-specific `normalizeGeminiBaseUrl` function, which could indicate that the old function was incorrectly normalizing the base URL for Gemini API endpoints, potentially causing malformed requests.

The new `gateway.tailscale.controlUrl` config field is missing corresponding `FIELD_LABELS` and `FIELD_HELP` entries, so it will appear in the settings UI without a label or help text, making it undiscoverable to users.

The Telegram extension directly imports from `src/infra/errors.js` in violation of the extension boundary rule, tightly coupling the extension to a core internal file path that could break resolution during packaging or refactoring.

The SKILL.md documentation incorrectly claims a newly installed skill is available "immediately — no restart needed," when in reality the CLI agent snapshots skills only at session start, meaning users in an active session must restart before the new skill is usable.

The new "ClawPool" plugin entry is inserted after "WeChat" in the community plugin list, violating the repository's documented rule requiring alphabetical ordering for docs lists, which makes the list harder to maintain and inconsistent with established standards.

Thinking events are only broadcast to run-scoped recipients registered at run start, so clients that reconnect or late-join a session mid-run have no way to subscribe to the in-flight reasoning stream until the next run begins.
A placeholder CI trigger entry ("do not merge") was accidentally added to the release changelog, which if shipped would publish misleading release notes to users.

Because `scheduleChatScroll()` runs and retries before the async Mermaid render pipeline finishes expanding the SVG, the chat view can end up scrolled short of the bottom, leaving the newest content below the viewport until the user scrolls manually.

The `resolveDiscordEffectiveRoute` mock returns `undefined` instead of a valid route, causing `resolveDiscordNativeChoiceContext` to fall into its error fallback and making the `/think` autocomplete test unreliable since it no longer validates the intended session override path.

When `OPENCLAW_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS` is set to a malformed value, the fallback to `OPENCLAW_TEST_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS` is silently skipped, causing handshake-timeout tests to use the 10s default instead of their intended short timeout, potentially making tests slow or flaky.
The `debugDrawerOpen` state variable is added but never read or used to toggle debug logging, leaving the debug connection logger permanently disabled and the feature non-functional.

A broken relative link in `docs/design/README.md` points outside the `docs/` directory to a non-existent `../../ROADMAP.md`, causing `pnpm docs:check-links` to fail and resulting in a broken "Companion file" link on the published docs page.

The SKILL.md frontmatter only defines a `uv`-based install path, causing `installSkill` to immediately fail on Linux/Windows hosts that lack `uv` or Homebrew, even though a standard `pip install operon-guard` method is available upstream.

The documentation misleadingly implies that `test` accepts arbitrary skill directory paths, but the underlying `load_agent_from_path()` uses `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location()`, which returns `None` for directories lacking an `__init__.py`, causing the command to silently fail before any verification runs.

The `auth.order` configuration example in the failover docs directs users to the wrong config file path (`~/.openclaw/gateway/config.json`) instead of the actual path OpenClaw reads (`~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`), causing multi-account rotation to silently fail even when the setting is configured correctly.

The pre-hook unit tests use hardcoded POSIX shell syntax that is incompatible with Windows' `cmd.exe`, risking CI failures on the supported Windows test matrix even when the underlying feature code is correct.

The new `"reuse"` cron session target was added to the TypeScript type but was not surfaced in the CLI's `cron add` or `cron edit` commands, making it impossible for operators to create or manage reuse jobs through the supported CLI workflow despite the backend already accepting it.

The Discord extension now directly imports `DiscordAccountConfig` and `DiscordActionConfig` from the external `openclaw/plugin-sdk/discord` package instead of routing them through the local `./runtime-api` barrel, violating the repository's extension SDK self-import guardrail and risking packaging and import drift.

Mermaid is initialized once without a theme setting, causing diagrams to always render with the default light theme even when the UI is in dark mode, resulting in visual inconsistency and potential low-contrast rendering.

When chat transcript search filters in or out messages containing Mermaid diagrams, `scheduleMermaidRender()` is never called because the search-driven rerender bypasses the tracked lifecycle fields, leaving newly inserted `.mermaid-block` elements blank until an unrelated update triggers rendering.

When Claude's `stream-json` mode emits an empty or missing `result` field, `parseCliJsonl` returns `null` instead of falling back to extract the actual reply text from earlier assistant/message events, causing the runner to display raw NDJSON output to the user.

Changing `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS` and `CLAWDBOT_SKIP_CHANNELS` from `1` to `0` in the `gateway:dev` script disables the intended channel-skipping behavior, causing real channel providers to start during local development instead of the lightweight dev mode, which can lead to startup errors and unintended external side effects.

The new `contextTotal` calculation in `grouped-render.ts` omits alternative cache-read token field aliases (e.g., `cached_tokens`, `prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens`) that are normalized elsewhere, causing the grouped chat footer to incorrectly display near-0% context occupancy for Moonshot/Kimi-style sessions that use those alternate field shapes.

The pre-poll value of `remainingMs` is passed to `readOpenAiBatchError` without being recomputed after the status poll completes, allowing the error-file download to block for nearly a full extra timeout window on failure paths instead of respecting the overall batch operation deadline.

Replacing `setImmediate` with a hard-coded 25ms `setTimeout` is an unreliable synchronization strategy for asserting asynchronous media upsert behavior, as it can still cause flaky tests on slower runners where the debounced async processing takes longer than the fixed delay.

The README falsely labels the community-built "OpenClaw Easy" desktop app as "Official," contradicting the project's own documentation and misleading users into treating an unsupported, third-party wrapper as the security-reviewed, first-party GUI client.

The `scope` field in the CONFIRM example is inconsistently typed as a bare string `"purchase"` instead of the structured object defined elsewhere in the spec, creating two incompatible wire shapes for the same field and losing the workflow/step binding that the rest of the document depends on.

The documentation defines two inconsistent type string casings ("confirm" vs "CONFIRM") for the same primitive, which would cause runtimes dispatching on literal event-type strings to treat them as different, non-interoperable protocol variants.

The protocol inconsistently defines takeover handling by specifying a `STOP` wire event with an optional `takeover_mode` field but separately listing an `on_takeover` callback with no normative rule connecting the two, leaving runtimes free to implement divergent behaviors that undermine the goal of stable hook points.

The `toolsExpanded` fallback in `renderChat()` uses only the global `verboseDefault` rather than respecting agent-specific verbose configuration, which can cause tool cards to incorrectly render as expanded even when the agent ran with verbose mode off.

The regression test for `resolveSubagentCompletionOrigin` is never executed by CI because the test runner only discovers `*.test.ts`, `*.live.test.ts`, and `*.e2e.test.ts` files, leaving the `.mjs` test file completely ignored and providing no actual protection against regressions.

The Zalo Personal link in the zh-CN channel index contains a duplicated locale prefix (`/zh-CN/zh-CN/channels/zalouser`) that causes it to resolve to a missing page instead of the correct translated page at `/zh-CN/channels/zalouser`.

The test for the "already pending request" case no longer mocks `upsertChannelPairingRequest` to return `created: false`, so the pending-request branch that suppresses duplicate pairing replies is never exercised and the `expectedSendCount: 1` assertion is invalid.

The `COPILOT_XHIGH_MODEL_IDS` allowlist was updated to include `gpt-5.4` but omits `gpt-5.4-mini`, meaning users on the `github-copilot/gpt-5.4-mini` model will incorrectly be denied xhigh thinking support despite being on the same GPT-5.4 rollout.

The `getProviderLabel()` function only lowercases provider IDs before lookup but lacks entries for real canonical IDs like `azure-openai-responses` and `openai-codex`, causing those providers to render as raw slugs in the UI instead of user-friendly group names.

The skill parse warning logs the file path and error message as structured metadata rather than inline in the message string, so in non-TTY compact logging mode operators will only see the generic warning text with no actionable details about which skill was dropped or why.

The delivery log incorrectly records the send mode as "reply" based solely on whether `sendReplyToMessageId` is set, even when the underlying Feishu API silently falls back to sending a top-level message due to the target being withdrawn or deleted, making reply-target failures harder to diagnose.

The fallback guard only suppresses duplicate final text replies for routed turns, causing normal ACP turns to always send a redundant final text reply after streamed block replies when TTS yields no media, resulting in duplicate output for users.

The Arabic locale file is missing the `languages.ar` key, causing the language selector to fail to resolve the Arabic language's own name when the UI is set to Arabic, leading to a fallback or raw key display and a failing registry test.

When launched from the Meson build directory, the relative path traversal to locate the OpenClaw CLI resolves incorrectly to `/dist/index.js` instead of the intended `<repo>/dist/index.js`, causing local dev testing to silently fall back to a wrong or missing CLI version.

The OTEL output text is captured from `replyPayloads` before later mutations (e.g., unscheduled-reminder guard, verbose notices, `appendUsageLine`) are applied, meaning the exported telemetry content will not match the actual user-visible reply when `diagnostics.otel.includeContent` is enabled.

When only total token count is known, defaulting `gen_ai.usage.input_tokens` and `gen_ai.usage.output_tokens` to `0` produces misleading span data that can skew dashboards and cost calculations, rather than omitting those split fields entirely.

Removing the CSS rule that hid `.content-header` on mobile causes chat session/model controls to render twice on screens narrower than 1100px — once in the content header and again in the topbar gear menu — wasting vertical space and breaking the mobile layout.

The dashboard header subtitle is missing `min-width: 0` on its flex item, preventing `text-overflow: ellipsis` from working correctly for long localized strings and causing layout overflow instead of clean text truncation.

Adding `export type * from "../infra/json-files.js"` is ineffective because the source module exports only functions and no named types, meaning any downstream consumers failing due to missing JSON-file types will continue to fail after this change.

Swapping the fallback priority to prefer `label` over `displayName` can cause raw session keys or query tokens to be displayed instead of human-readable names when a user has saved the session key as the label, directly undermining the change's intended goal.

The slash-model-ID fallback logic only searches `cfg.agents.defaults.models` for candidate providers, so it fails to resolve bare Ollama model IDs (e.g. `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B`) in `allowAny` configurations where providers are discovered solely from the live gateway catalog, causing valid models to be incorrectly rejected as "not allowed."

Using `trim()` on incremental thinking delta chunks strips meaningful boundary whitespace before diffing, causing spaces and newlines between tokens to be lost in the accumulated reasoning stream output.

In summary-first transcripts, pre-compaction assistant messages with missing or unparsable timestamps bypass both stale-usage-clearing checks, leaving inflated usage counts in the TUI context counter because the logic only handles assistants with valid timestamps or positional ordering relative to the compaction marker.

The raw comparison of `everyMs` and `anchorMs` fields ignores the floor/coerce normalization applied elsewhere, so a job with a fractional interval like `60000.9` will be falsely detected as changed, triggering a next-run recomputation that can silently skip an execution even though the effective schedule is unchanged.

The minimum poll stall threshold of 31 seconds (30s long-poll + 1s buffer) is too tight, as it leaves insufficient margin for network latency, causing healthy polling sessions on slow or high-latency connections to trigger false "polling stall detected" restarts.

When a debug `[TUI] evt=...` line arrives as a `delta` event before a hidden `final` message, the streamed fallback path persists the unfiltered debug text into `chatMessages` because `state.chatStream` is never sanitized of such lines, allowing debug output to render in the UI.

Using `100vh` for the modal's max-height is unsafe on mobile browsers where dynamic toolbars cause the layout viewport to exceed the visible area, potentially keeping action buttons clipped off-screen; `100dvh` should be used instead to match the rest of the UI's mobile-safe sizing pattern.

The `deliverWebReply()` function only passes `replyToId` in its options object and never extracts or forwards `mentionedJids`, so any `@mention` targets in AI-generated replies sent through WhatsApp groups will silently render as plain text instead of triggering proper mention notifications.

The new TUI `/model status` handler short-circuits to a minimal `provider/model` string from local state instead of forwarding the command to the gateway, which already returns richer status information including runtime model, defaults, auth profiles, and per-provider details — making the output incomplete and potentially misleading when fallback models or auth overrides are in effect.

Using `listChannelPluginCatalogEntries` with workspace discovery in `loadBundledConfigSchemaResponse` allows local developer overrides to take priority over bundled metadata, making the docs baseline nondeterministic and risking the publication of non-bundled channel labels/blurbs.

Adding `fill="none"` to the `arrowDown` icon template has no effect because the only live usage already applies `fill: none` via CSS, meaning the triangle artifact bug remains unfixed and the change should instead target the scroll pill's styling or path data directly.

Replacing the mock-based fault injection with `fs.chmod(..., 0o500)` makes the sweeper failure test non-deterministic on Windows CI, where that chmod call does not reliably deny write access, potentially causing the test to hang instead of hitting the intended error path.

Adding `readonly` to the plugin property does not fully fix the lazy-loading issue because `registry.ts` still eagerly maps over `bundledWebSearchPluginRegistrations` at module scope, meaning a plugin caught in a circular-import/TDZ window will still throw during initialization before the intended lazy resolution can occur.

Because the Copilot extension registers an empty model list, the `gpt-5.3-codex` forward-compat alias almost always falls through to a generic synthetic model instead of cloning the codex template's metadata, silently losing template-derived behavior.

The OpenAI-completions Ollama compat transport only forwards `num_ctx` from `model.options` instead of the full options record, causing newly supported parameters like `top_k`, `top_p`, and `repeat_penalty` to silently have no effect on compat runs despite working correctly on the native Ollama transport.

Using `importOriginal` in the `openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-runtime` mock inadvertently re-imports the full reply runtime module graph (including the heavy `reply.js` stack), negating the performance isolation the mock was intended to provide and risking OOM failures on low-memory Vitest lanes when only `HEARTBEAT_TOKEN` and a stubbed `getReplyFromConfig` are actually needed.
A capitalization-only edit to CHANGELOG.md inadvertently drops the Telegram/topics bullet point, causing the DM auto-topic-labeling feature from #51502 to be omitted from release notes entirely.

When `totalTokensFresh` is undefined (e.g., on legacy session rows), coercing it to `false` in `/context json` creates an internally contradictory response where a non-null `totalTokens` is paired with `totalTokensFresh: false`, diverging from the `sessions --json` command which treats missing freshness as fresh.

Although `totalTokensEstimate` is now correctly included in the JSON output, the human-readable `status` table still reads only from `sess.totalTokens` (which is `freshTotal ?? null`), so sessions with `totalTokensFresh === false` will continue to display as `unknown` even when a valid estimate is available.

Because `gateway.handshakeTimeoutMs` is classified as restart-only in the reload plan and hot-reload gateways silently drop restart-required changes, updating this config key at runtime will have no effect until the gateway is manually restarted.

During prewarm, `needsTranscriptUsageFallback` is evaluated without the runtime subagent model (unlike `buildGatewaySessionRow` which uses `subagentRun?.model`), causing restored subagent sessions to be incorrectly skipped during cache warming and re-incurring transcript-read latency on the first real call to `sessions.list`.

When a user resets their model selection to "Default," `rememberResolvedChatModelRecent` is called unconditionally, causing the resolved default model to be incorrectly recorded as an explicit recent choice and pushed to the top of the recents list, displacing the user's actual selections.

When a session reports zero token usage after a previously nonzero session, the code ignores the freshly-computed `prevFresh` flag and incorrectly restores the old non-zero prompt size instead of persisting the explicit zero, causing stale context counts to be displayed until a positive snapshot arrives.

The CLI instructions documented in SKILL.md require navigating into a source checkout directory, but since the skill directs users to install via PyPI (`pip install tradingagents`), the documented CLI flow will fail because no local source tree exists and users should instead be directed to use the `tradingagents` console script entry point exposed by the installed package.

Adding `docs/zh-CN/README.md` outside the established translation pipeline means future updates to the canonical root `README.md` will never be automatically propagated to this file, causing the translation to drift out of sync almost immediately.

The Chinese README's navigation links use `../...` relative paths that escape the `docs/zh-CN/` directory and resolve to English documentation pages instead of the existing zh-CN equivalents, breaking the localized onboarding experience for Chinese readers.
The newly added `zh-CN/README.md` translation is orphaned because no existing navigation configs or entry points reference it, making it unreachable to users browsing the Chinese documentation.

The import path rewrite from a relative `../../src/plugin-sdk/telegram.js` to `openclaw/plugin-sdk/telegram` will cause the plugin-sdk boundary test suite to fail because the associated guardrail baselines and fixture allowlists still reference the old relative import path and were not updated in tandem.

The new try/catch around `resolveMediaList()` doesn't fix the intended Discord CDN 404 failures (which are already handled internally per-attachment) and instead silently drops the entire media list when unexpected resolver bugs or malformed payloads occur.

The test mocks the entire `resolveMediaList()` helper to reject rather than testing the real fetch-failure path, meaning it only validates that an outer catch swallows exceptions and could pass even if actual Discord attachment fetch failures are mishandled deeper in the production code.

The `isHeading()` function incorrectly identifies `#` characters inside fenced or indented code blocks as Markdown headings, causing `headingAware` chunking to split documents in the middle of code examples rather than at true section boundaries.

Making the `node` parameter optional for the `describe` action while reusing a canvas-only default node resolver means that non-canvas nodes will still throw an error when no node is explicitly specified, even though `node.describe` supports any connected node type.

Marking the `openclaw` peer dependency as optional in `peerDependenciesMeta` bypasses npm's version compatibility enforcement during plugin installation, allowing users on incompatible older OpenClaw releases to install the plugin without error and only encounter broken `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` imports at runtime.

Setting `overflow-x: auto` on `.daily-chart-bars` causes vertically-positioned bar tooltips to be clipped and unreadable, since browsers treat overflow as hidden in all directions when any overflow axis is set to `auto`.

When `OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR` is unset, the script unconditionally overwrites `agents.defaults.workspace` with a default path on every restart, silently reverting any user-configured workspace overrides and contradicting the script's own "preserves explicit user overrides" guarantee.

A generated runtime log file containing operational metadata, timestamps, and internal diagnostic paths was accidentally committed to version control, where it adds noise, bloats repo size, and risks exposing ephemeral system details.

The regression test for orphaned embedded `tool_result` pairs never triggers the pruning logic because the fixture transcript is too small to exceed the compaction budget, meaning the test fails to exercise the intended code path and would pass even on a broken implementation.

The new changelog entry was prepended to the top of the `### Changes` section instead of appended to the end, violating the repository's documented ordering convention and increasing the risk of merge conflicts.

The immediate post-compaction AGENTS.md reinjection into the retry's system prompt does not suppress the existing end-of-turn reinjection path, causing the same ~3 KB context block to be injected twice — once during the retry and again on the next external turn — resulting in avoidable prompt bloat that can trigger unnecessary compaction on smaller-context models.

Adding a glossary translation that maps the literal tool identifier `exec` to `exec 执行` will cause automated i18n scripts to incorrectly translate the term in headings and labels, making translated documentation diverge from the actual command name users must type.

Using `--init-file` with `-c` in non-interactive bash invocations silently ignores the specified shell profile, causing any variables, aliases, or functions defined in `commands.shellProfile` to be unavailable at runtime despite the setting appearing to be accepted.

Running a full `pnpm build` (including network-dependent `npm install` steps for bundled plugins) inside every PR test shard unnecessarily multiplies CI fragility, as a single transient registry failure can block all 6 shards before any unit tests run, when only a narrow subset of build artifacts is actually needed.

The nullish-coalescing chain short-circuits when a top-level `plan_name`/`plan` field is already present, causing `modelName` to never be appended and producing an ambiguous "Coding Plan" label instead of the intended "Coding Plan · MiniMax-M*" for payloads that contain both `model_remains` and a plan field.

The skill's example commands hardcode a relative `scripts/` path instead of using the `{baseDir}` variable, causing them to fail with "No such file or directory" when the skill is invoked from the workspace or repo root as intended.

Changing the install hint from the bare spec `acpx` to the scoped package `@openclaw/acpx-plugin` breaks the bundled plugin fast path, causing the install command to fail on offline or restricted-network hosts even when the ACP plugin is already present on disk.

The documentation incorrectly requires provider IDs to match plugin IDs, which conflicts with OpenClaw's existing support for single plugins owning multiple provider IDs and would force authors into incorrect configurations or unnecessary package splits.

The documentation incorrectly implies that a channel's ID must match the plugin ID, when in reality the loader, registry, and manifest treat them as independent identifiers, which will mislead plugin authors—especially those whose channel ID differs from their plugin ID or who register multiple channels.

An unclosed ```json5 code fence in the Tailscale documentation will cause all subsequent Markdown content to be rendered as part of the code block, breaking the page structure and making the rest of the guidance unreadable in generated docs.

Adding `allow_sending_without_reply: true` without also including `message_thread_id` means that if the original message is deleted, the file-too-large warning will be sent as a standalone message to the chat's default thread instead of the correct forum or DM topic.

The documentation incorrectly claims that `chattr +i` is root-proof, when in reality a privileged process can simply remove the immutable bit before modifying the file, giving users a false sense of security.

The hardened template's overly broad refusal gate blocks legitimate first-person drafting workflows by triggering on any "adopt" or "simulate" language, rather than narrowing the condition to only unauthorized identity-override or exfiltration attempts.

The README includes an unsubstantiated "~95% attack-blocking" effectiveness claim with no benchmark, test corpus, or reproducible methodology to back it up, which could give operators a false sense of security and lead to poor risk decisions.

Incorrectly including editable workspace files like `AGENTS.md` and `TOOLS.md` in the bootstrap denylist will block routine agent maintenance updates and cause unnecessary boundary violations in normal OpenClaw workflows.

The AGENTS.md template change incorrectly forces a no-op on every heartbeat when HEARTBEAT.md is missing, breaking the documented proactive-check behavior for manually bootstrapped workspaces that rely on the missing-file path to trigger default attention checks.

When only `OPENCLAW_TUI_USER_BG` is set without a corresponding `OPENCLAW_TUI_USER_TEXT` override, the mismatched default text color from the base palette can produce near-unreadable contrast ratios against the custom background, particularly across light/dark mode boundaries.

A new regression test expects `mentionedBot` to be `false` for `@_all` messages, but the underlying `checkBotMentioned()` logic in `bot-content.ts` still unconditionally returns `true` for `@_all`, meaning the test will deterministically fail until the parser fix is shipped alongside it.

Removing the retry logic around `guest_powershell` and `guest_exec` calls in the Windows install phases means a single transient Parallels guest-exec failure after snapshot restore will permanently fail the smoke test instead of recovering via `wait_for_guest_ready()` and retrying.

The new allowlist warning inaccurately states that name-based channel keys will never route successfully, omitting two valid runtime paths—startup name-to-ID resolution and `dangerouslyAllowNameMatching`—where they can still match, which could mislead operators during troubleshooting.

When the EPLB async worker thread crashes mid-rebalance, the exception is silently logged and swallowed with no notification to the main thread, causing `rebalanced=True` to remain set permanently across all EP ranks via `all_reduce` coordination, which permanently blocks all future `rearrange()` calls and disables load balancing indefinitely without raising any error.

The `GlmAsrEncoder` incorrectly initializes the fused QKV bias with `bias=True` for all projections, but since the HuggingFace checkpoint omits `k_proj.bias`, the K-bias shard is never written and retains uninitialized garbage values from `torch.empty()`, causing non-deterministic and incorrect model outputs.

The gRPC server registers SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers only after the potentially minutes-long `AsyncLLM.from_vllm_config()` call, so any signal received during engine initialization triggers Python's default hard termination, bypassing the `finally` block and orphaning child processes instead of invoking graceful cleanup via `async_llm.shutdown()`.
The `vllm-xpu-kernels` dependency was updated to version `v0.1.4` but incorrectly points to the Python 3.8 wheel instead of the required Python 3.12 wheel, which will break the build.

In `vllm/utils/cache.py`, `LRUCache.touch()` silently inserts non-existent keys into the internal LRU order structure without adding them to the cache data, creating "ghost keys" that cause the eviction loop to hang indefinitely when the cache exceeds capacity.
The fix in `nemotron_parse.py` intended to correct weight loading introduces a new bug by using integer shard IDs where string identifiers are expected, which could cause a crash.

In vllm's XBO mode, both `gpu_worker.py` and `xpu_worker.py` incorrectly initialize the workspace manager using DBO-only logic (`2 if enable_dbo else 1`) instead of the actual `parallel_config.num_ubatches`, while the `is_last_ubatch_empty` guard in `ubatch_utils.py` fails to prevent empty ubatch slice creation when `ubatch_size > num_tokens`, together causing assertion crashes and workspace sizing mismatches for valid configurations like `--ubatch-size 50 --data-parallel-size 2`.

This GPU kernel refactoring improves readability by introducing helper functions and removing verbose comments, but critically risks correctness issues by removing a conditional compilation path that handled cases where matrix A exceeds LDS capacity, and by removing a scheduling barrier that may be necessary to prevent race conditions in DPP wave operations.

In multi-node Ray data-parallel deployments, the KV transfer `engine_id` is suffixed with the node-local rank (`local_index`) instead of the global DP rank (`index`), causing ID collisions across nodes that silently corrupt KV transfers by routing them to the wrong workers with incorrect topology parameters.

The code review identifies a critical vulnerability in `ParsableContext` where an `IndexError` and server crash can occur if no response messages are parsed, despite the overall fix for deferred parsing of incomplete streaming deltas being sound.

The XPU weight processing logic contains a redundant no-op assignment and an unconditional zero-point transpose that incorrectly applies to the new GPTQ Marlin quantization path, potentially corrupting model weights during quantization.

The `MooncakeConnector.get_finished()` method ignores the `finished_req_ids` parameter and passes nothing to the underlying worker, violating the `KVConnectorBase_V1` contract by returning all finished request IDs without filtering against scheduler-acknowledged requests, which causes crashes (assertion failures or KeyErrors) when requests are aborted mid-transfer.

In `reduce_scatter`, using `movedim(0, dim)` instead of `movedim(dim, 0)` before calling PyTorch's `reduce_scatter_tensor` causes the scatter and reduction to be applied to the wrong axis for `dim >= 2`, silently producing corrupted outputs with incorrect shapes.

The `skip_prompt_length_check` flag in `_validate_prompt_len()` unconditionally skips all prompt validation for encoder-decoder models like Whisper, allowing overlong user-supplied decoder prompts to bypass length checks and produce negative `max_tokens`, resulting in silent failures or empty output.

After the final safetensors shard is processed in `safetensors_weights_iterator`, any remaining entries in `leftover_state_dict` containing partially reconstructed torchao tensor subclass data are silently discarded without validation, allowing incomplete checkpoints to load without error and causing models to produce incorrect inference results.

The `rebalance_experts` method's return type hint incorrectly declares a `tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]` when the implementation actually returns a single `torch.Tensor`, breaking the contract with `AbstractEplbPolicy` and its callers.

When speculative decoding is batched with prefill tokens, the `a` and `b` gating tensors are not indexed by `spec_token_indx` before being passed to `fused_sigmoid_gating_delta_rule_update`, causing the kernel to receive mismatched gating parameters and produce incorrect GDN attention outputs.

In `mamba_attn.py`, the persistent buffer for prefix-cached block indices is sliced using `num_decode_tokens` (a per-token count) instead of `num_decodes` (a per-request count), causing stale or uninitialized entries to be returned during speculative decoding where `num_decode_tokens > num_decodes`, which corrupts Mamba state cache lookups and can produce incorrect model outputs.

The JSON fallback parsing for `tool_choice="required"` uses overly broad exception suppression that hides unexpected errors and allows partial tool call results to be committed if an exception occurs mid-loop, which can silently corrupt output by clearing original content while only retaining incomplete tool calls.

In `ModelOptFp8PbWoLinearMethod.process_weights_after_loading`, the kernel is stored as `self.w8a8_block_fp8_linear` but the subsequent `hasattr` check incorrectly references `self.fp8_linear`, causing the guard to always be false and silently skipping critical ROCm FP8 weight padding and MI300/gfx94 scale normalization steps entirely.
The NFS path detection logic incorrectly constructs the root mount point prefix as `//` instead of `/`, causing NFS filesystem detection to fail for any path residing on a root-level NFS mount.

The `page_stride` calculation incorrectly uses `nixl_agent_meta.block_lens[0]` for all layers instead of indexing with the current layer index, which will produce wrong strides for MLA models where block lengths differ between layers.

The refactored `pixel_shuffle` implementation incorrectly uses `torch.Tensor.view()` in two places, which will cause a definite runtime error in one case and a potential memory-layout bug in the other, and should be replaced with `reshape()` to safely achieve the intended performance improvements.

The refactored `extract_tool_calls_streaming` method emits an opening brace `{` to start the JSON arguments object but is missing the corresponding logic to emit the closing brace `}` when the function call ends, which will cause malformed JSON to be streamed to the client.

The reset logic for handling async KV load failures incorrectly updates `num_external_computed_tokens`, which can result in a negative value and cause subsequent errors in requests with shared prefixes.

The `_copy_loop` background thread in `DmaCopyBackend` has no exception handling around `copy_blocks()`, so any CUDA transfer failure silently kills the daemon thread without recording a completion event, causing affected requests to hang indefinitely while the process continues accepting new ones.

The pull request replaces the unsafe `eval()` function with a secure AST-based expression evaluator that uses an allowlist of safe arithmetic operations, mitigating the risk of arbitrary code execution via prompt injection.

Removing the `use_marlin` conditional causes `GPTQMarlinLinearMethod` to always be instantiated even when the Marlin kernel is unsupported, likely resulting in a crash instead of gracefully returning `None` to fall back to an unquantized layer.

A duplicate assignment sets `result_msg["reasoning"]` twice with the same value, while the intended `result_msg["reasoning_content"]` compatibility key is never set, likely breaking backward compatibility.

The IndexCache feature for DeepSeek Attention models contains a critical bug in the `index_topk_freq` logic that causes top-k computations to be skipped at incorrect intervals, leading to wrong sparse attention behavior during inference.

In the JAIS model, ALiBi positional encoding was being applied unconditionally regardless of the configured `position_embedding_type`, causing positional encodings to be applied twice when using `learned` embeddings, and the fix resolves this by gating ALiBi slope computation behind a check for `position_embedding_type == 'alibi'`.

The `local_rank` assigned to each Ray executor worker is derived from worker enumeration order rather than the worker's assigned GPU position within the sorted `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES` list, causing workers to select the wrong GPU and potentially triggering OOM errors or memory conflicts in multi-GPU environments.
The new `make_fusion_pass` factory has a critical cache invalidation bug that could cause stale or incorrect fusion pass behavior.

The generated fusion pass's `uuid` method omits `preprocessors` from its hash calculation, meaning changes to preprocessors won't invalidate Inductor's cache and can cause stale, incorrect compiled artifacts to be used.

The variable swap intended to exchange `reasoning` and `final_content` is a no-op because the assignment `reasoning, final_content = reasoning, final_content` assigns each variable back to itself, meaning the swap never actually occurs and the values remain unchanged.
The `aliases` subcommand is declared in `main.py` but lacks an `AliasesCommand` implementation in `local.py`, causing a `RuntimeError` when `vllm aliases` is invoked.

In `Gemma4Model.forward`, `_maybe_add_hidden_state` is called with relative layer indices instead of absolute layer indices, causing the wrong hidden states to be silently captured during EAGLE-3 speculative decoding on non-first pipeline-parallel ranks where `start_layer > 0`.

Replacing `cuda_device_count_stateless()` with `torch.accelerator.device_count()` introduces a compatibility break with PyTorch versions older than 2.4, where `torch.accelerator` does not exist and will raise an `AttributeError` at runtime.

The OLMo Hybrid model fails to load with TP=4 or TP=8 because `linear_num_value_heads=30` is not evenly divisible by those tensor parallel sizes, causing a cryptic `AssertionError` in `A_log` initialization while `dt_bias` silently truncates heads via floor division, and no upfront config validation exists to catch or clearly report this incompatibility.

When a cached block in `tel_safe_queue` gets a cache hit and `touch()` is called, the method incorrectly assumes all free blocks are in `free_block_queue` and will crash trying to remove the block from the wrong queue, requiring `touch()` to be updated to check `block.is_tel_safe` and remove from the correct queue using an O(1)-capable data structure.

The condition enabling the `ScoreRequest` endpoint on the SageMaker entrypoint omits the `token_embed` task check, which means late-interaction models relying on that task will not have the score API enabled, breaking functionality that works correctly in other entrypoints.
The new `_diff_and_emit_args` logic in `KimiK2ToolParser` fails to handle non-monotonic argument changes, which could corrupt JSON on the client side.

The JSON fallback parsing logic in `responses_parser.py` uses overly broad exception suppression and non-atomic parsing, which could cause silent failures and incorrect processing of partially valid tool call data.

Setting `evicted_groups=None` in `prepare_store` causes HMA-aware routing to incorrectly assume full eviction for offloaded blocks, severely degrading KV cache offloading effectiveness until the `OffloadingManager` is updated to support `BlockHashWithGroupId` for proper group ID extraction.
In `KimiLinear`'s new `load_weights` implementation, an unnecessary and confusing prefix removal is performed, which could lead to incorrect weight loading behavior.
In `vllm/transformers_utils/configs/olmo_hybrid.py`, the new `transformers` v5 validation path is skipped due to incorrect order of operations, which could result in model misconfiguration.

Replacing `cuda_device_count_stateless()` with `torch.accelerator.device_count()` without a fallback will cause an `AttributeError` on PyTorch versions older than 2.4, which do not have the `torch.accelerator` module.

Adding the `resampy` dependency will trigger an `AttributeError` in audio-related tests because `vllm/multimodal/audio.py` uses `np.floating`, which was removed in NumPy 2.0, while the environment pins `numpy==2.2.6`.

The updated `vllm_xpu_kernels` wheel in `requirements/xpu.txt` specifies a Python 3.8 (`cp38`) build, which is incompatible with the Python 3.12 environment used in `docker/Dockerfile.xpu`, causing installation to fail.

The `_initialize_fi_ar_workspaces` function silently returns `False` on initialization failure instead of raising an exception, risking the program continuing with an uninitialized workspace and causing incorrect results or crashes.

In the modular TRTLLM MXFP4 expert class, the `routing_method_type` is hardcoded instead of using the configured value, which could cause incorrect routing behavior inconsistent with the intended configuration.

The `_exceeds_logprobs_budget` check is skipped when `current_logprobs_sum` is 0, allowing the first request in a batch to exceed `max_logprobs_budget` if its `req_logprobs_cost` alone surpasses the limit.

In `unpack_tensor`, oversized tensors only trigger a warning instead of raising a `ValueError` like `packed_ipc_producer` does, which risks silently proceeding with unexpectedly large allocations that could go unnoticed and cause hard-to-debug failures.

A precision mismatch in fused RMS norm quantization kernels on gfx90a hardware, caused by a compiler optimization removing a necessary precision truncation, is fixed by changing an intermediate variable's type from `float` to `scalar_t` to make the truncation explicit, with an additional suggestion to restore the `const` qualifier on the modified variable declarations.
A personal log file (`DAILY_LEARNINGS.md`) containing sensitive information has been inadvertently included in the pull request and must be removed before merging.

The code review identifies a critical bug in the audio chunking logic that could cause a `TypeError`, while also suggesting improvements to the audio loading mechanism for better compatibility with various video formats.

In `gpu_model_runner.py`, accessing the device attribute directly on `raw_pooler_output` could cause an `AttributeError` crash if it is a list instead of a tensor, requiring a more robust device-retrieval approach.

A DCP + FP8 KV cache validation assertion is placed only inside the decode path, allowing pure prefill (chunked context) batches to bypass the guard and crash deep in a CUDA kernel with a dtype mismatch instead of failing with a clear error message.

An `IndexError` in multi-node distributed inference was caused by incorrect indexing into a sliced CPU ID list, and is fixed by using the modulo operator to calculate the correct local index, with a suggestion to add boundary checks for better error handling on misconfiguration.

The `register_custom_kv_cache_specs` method is missing the `@classmethod` decorator, meaning it is incorrectly defined as an instance method despite being intended for class-level invocation, which will cause incorrect behavior when called on the class.

The GLM-4 tool parser had a double serialization bug in streaming responses where tool call arguments were incorrectly stored as a parsed dictionary instead of a raw JSON string, causing arguments to be serialized twice.

The original `kv_bytes_per_block` calculation incorrectly multiplied by `world_size`, causing an underestimation of CPU-offloadable blocks, and removing this factor corrects the calculation to properly utilize CPU offload memory.

Without explicitly synchronizing `_is_terminated` from `self.matcher.is_terminated()`, the `XgrammarGrammar` object may retain stale termination state, causing unexpected behavior or crashes when the underlying matcher has already terminated.

Missing boundary checks on `omp_cpuids_list` before index access could cause cryptic `IndexError` crashes when `VLLM_CPU_OMP_THREADS_BIND` is misconfigured, instead of surfacing a clear, actionable error message to the user.

A critical contradiction exists where the PR claims to enable mxfp4 LoRA on ROCm, but the code still raises a `NotImplementedError`, meaning the feature remains unimplemented despite the stated intent.

A fix is applied to the Responses API to prevent a crash when `tool_choice="required"` and the model output exceeds `max_output_tokens` by suppressing `ValidationError` exceptions during JSON parsing, ensuring no tool call is returned when the output is invalid or truncated.

An IndexError in CPUWorker during distributed inference was caused by incorrectly using a global rank to index a sliced list of CPU IDs, and was fixed by calculating the correct local index within the sliced list to prevent out-of-bounds access.

The assertion `remainder > 0` is overly strict and should be `remainder >= 0`, because a valid `groups_ss=0` configuration produces a remainder of 0, which would incorrectly fail the assertion and break models that decompose evenly into `intermediate_size` alone.

Replacing `torch.Tensor.view` with `torch.Tensor.reshape` in `FP8ScaledMMLinearKernel` fixes a correctness issue where non-contiguous tensors would cause a runtime error, making the kernel more robust to varying input memory layouts.

The pull request fixes a PyTorch stable ABI compatibility issue by correctly changing `torch::stable::Tensor` parameters in the `permute_cols` function from pass-by-reference to pass-by-value (as required for trivially copyable types), and also resolves build failures by adding missing CUTLASS directory includes for the `_C_stable_libtorch` target in `CMakeLists.txt`.

The T-LRU eviction tests have inverted names and docstrings that directly contradict their actual assertions, and the behavior verified in one test conflicts with the documentation in `docs/design/tlru_caching.md` regarding how a large `ξ` value affects TEL-safe block counts, indicating a deeper inconsistency between the implementation, tests, and documentation that must be resolved.
The new 'pooling' test jobs in the refactored CI configuration are missing a required dependency installation step, which could cause the pooling suite to fail at runtime.

The PR refactors MoE dispatch logic to properly handle quantized vs. unquantized data by correctly setting `scale_dim` to 0 for unquantized dispatch, adding a guard against silent failures when `prepare_finalize` is unexpectedly `None`, and a reviewer suggests also explicitly zeroing out `scale_type_size` when `scale_dim` is 0 to avoid a misleading non-zero value being used in unquantized paths.

An overly broad `except Exception: pass` block silently swallows all exceptions during the CUDA device capability check, hiding unrelated critical errors that should instead be caught specifically (e.g., `RuntimeError`) and logged for debugging.

The `source_file_dependencies` list for the DeepEP MoE Test CI step is incomplete, missing several paths that the test actually depends on, which means code changes to those paths won't trigger the test and could allow broken code to be merged undetected.

The `evicted_groups` field in the `BlockRemoved` KV cache event is not populated for blocks evicted through offloading managers (ARC and LRU), limiting the effectiveness of HMA-aware prefix-cache routing in KV cache offloading scenarios.

Logprobs were being silently dropped in streaming chat completions when chunks contained only tool-call control tokens and no message delta, because the condition for skipping empty chunks failed to account for the presence of logprobs.

The `--enable-ep-weight-filter` flag lacks a validation check to ensure `enable_expert_parallel` is also active, which could lead to silent failures if the flag is used without its required dependency.
The type hint for the optional bias parameter in the new custom op is incorrect, which could cause runtime errors when `GateLinear` is used without a bias.
In `rejection_sampler.py`, a generated random number could be zero, introducing a slight bias, and should be clamped or offset to ensure it is always positive.

Changing `agent_pool` to `mi325_2` leaves the job label and `mirror_hardwares` list referencing the old MI250 hardware, creating a misleading mismatch that could cause confusion or incorrect job routing.
The streaming parser incorrectly normalizes text chunks independently, causing unreliable behavior when malformed paths are split across multiple streaming chunks.

The `sampler_workspace` buffer is redundantly allocated in `GPUModelRunner.__init__` outside of the memory profiler's accounting, potentially wasting significant GPU memory, and an unused parameter in `Sampler.forward` adds unnecessary confusion to the code.

The change sets `cpp.dynamic_threads` to `True` in the CPU platform's `torch.compile` inductor configuration so that thread counts are passed as dynamic arguments to compiled kernels rather than hardcoded, preventing OpenMP thread reallocation from breaking thread binding.

The `rebalance_experts` method in `flashlb.py` declares `old_global_expert_indices` as a required `torch.Tensor` instead of the optional `torch.Tensor | None` defined in the base class `AbstractEplbPolicy`, violating the Liskov Substitution Principle and causing a signature incompatibility.

The hardcoded `constexpr TopK = 2048` value lacks configurability or documentation, which could cause silent correctness failures if the actual required TopK differs across use cases without any compile-time or runtime mechanism to validate or override it.

The `paddleocr_vl_patch_hf_runner` function permanently modifies global `sys.modules` state without cleanup, which can break test isolation by causing unexpected side effects in other tests running in the same process that rely on the original `create_causal_mask` behavior.

The CI configuration for the Hybrid SSM NixlConnector PD accuracy test targets the wrong GPU architecture by specifying `agent_pool: mi250_4` (MI250/gfx90a), despite the test being validated on gfx950 and intended for gfx942 (MI300 series), meaning the test will not provide meaningful coverage for the target hardware.

The `hf_comparison` test for `LiquidAI/LFM2-ColBERT-350M` will fail because `trust_remote_code` is set to `False` instead of `True`, which is required to load this model's custom architecture code from Hugging Face.

The newly added unit test contains incorrect test data and reimplements logic instead of testing the module directly, causing it to fail for valid cases and provide a false sense of correctness despite the underlying implementation fix being sound.

A temporary hack sets `is_nvfp4_scale_swizzled` based on the backend to work around the TRTLLM kernel's inability to accept swizzled input quant scales, introducing design debt that needs a proper fix once MoE kernels manage their own quant configs.

A redundant `sampler_workspace` tensor is allocated outside the memory-profiled `load_model` context, wasting GPU memory because the correctly profiled allocation already occurs inside `load_model` on `self.sampler.sampler_workspace`.
A race condition in `libsndfile` error handling is fixed by recognizing `0` as a valid error code, with a suggestion to make the set of error codes immutable for improved robustness.

Casting `SingletonInstances.size()` (a `size_t`) to `int64_t` in the bounds check can overflow if the vector exceeds 2^63 - 1 elements, potentially wrapping to a negative value and causing incorrect comparisons; instead, `handle` should be cast to `size_t` after confirming it is non-negative.

The code review identifies two robustness issues in the tcmalloc integration: overly broad exception handling during the library search at build time, and unreliable version sorting when selecting the tcmalloc library at runtime.

Adding `ROCM_EXTRA_ARGS` to disable prefix caching and limit max sequences to one stabilizes a flaky ROCm embedding parity test, but undermines the `test_batch_parity` test's purpose by effectively disabling the batch processing it is meant to validate.
The `match` string passed to `pytest.raises` is incomplete, weakening the test assertion and potentially allowing unrelated exceptions with partially matching messages to incorrectly pass the test.

The `get_num_new_matched_tokens` function violates command-query separation by modifying the `request` object as a side effect, which is unexpected and misleading for a function whose name implies a read-only "get" operation.

A self-described "HACK" comment in the dummy LoRA creation logic for packed modules signals a fragile, poorly-understood workaround for mismatched `n_slices` and `len(replacements)` values that lacks sufficient explanation and risks future breakage or confusion during maintenance.

The PR correctly fixes a tokenization bug in `SamplingParams.update_from_tokenizer` but should not be merged until the accidentally committed temporary file `gsm8k_comment.txt` is removed from the repository.

A new `awq_linear` custom op is introduced to prevent Dynamo from statically caching a dynamic branching condition during `torch.compile()`, ensuring the correct AWQ quantization kernel is dispatched at runtime based on input size.

Thread-safe tokenizer caching logic is duplicated between `Hermes2ProToolParser` and `FunctionGemmaToolParser` instead of being abstracted into the shared `ToolParser` base class, reducing maintainability and increasing the risk of inconsistent concurrency handling.

The `except Exception` clause is too broad and should be narrowed to `except (SyntaxError, ValueError)` to avoid accidentally masking unrelated errors like `KeyboardInterrupt` or other system-level exceptions.

A `pytest.mark.skip` with a reason specific to `PerceptronAI/Isaac-0.1` is applied to the entire `isaac` test configuration, which also covers `PerceptronAI/Isaac-0.2-2B-Preview`, potentially skipping healthy tests unnecessarily if only one model is broken.

Silently swallowing exceptions with a bare `pass` in the tokenizer decode block can hide underlying errors, and should instead log a warning to aid in debugging when prompt text unexpectedly cannot be decoded.

The patch generalizes ROCm vision encoder SDPA forcing and increases relative tolerance for test assertions, but the patching logic may need to be made more robust to safely handle diverse model structures without errors.

The hardcoded 150MiB redundancy buffer in `gpu_worker.py` may be too large for memory-constrained GPUs or too small for very large models, and should instead be made configurable (e.g., via `CacheConfig`) to allow users to tune it for their specific hardware and workload.
The `match` string passed to `pytest.raises` is incomplete, making the test less robust by failing to verify the full or sufficiently specific expected error message.

The import of `_use_lazy_graph_module` from `torch.fx._lazy_graph_module` depends on a private PyTorch API that could be changed or removed without notice in future versions, risking unexpected breakage.

The CI configuration for the new DeepEP MoE Buildkite test group has an incomplete `source_file_dependencies` list, which could prevent the test from being triggered when dependent files are modified, allowing potential breakages to go undetected on AMD hardware.

The code catches the broad `Exception` class instead of specific exceptions like `SyntaxError` and `ValueError`, which can inadvertently suppress unexpected errors and make error handling less robust.

File include directives in the documentation use Windows-style backslashes as path separators, which will break on Linux/macOS and should be replaced with forward slashes for cross-platform compatibility.

A magic number `6` is used to update `device_index` in the IPC args tuple without any explanation, and the previously existing comment clarifying that index 6 corresponds to the `device_index` argument of `torch.multiprocessing.reductions.rebuild_cuda_tensor` should be restored to maintain code readability.

A bug fix correctly enables `ParallelLMHead` quantization in `CompressedTensorsConfig`, though similar logic in `AWQConfig` and `GPTQConfig` may have the same gap and could warrant a follow-up update for consistency.
The `const` qualifier was removed from the `out_norm` variable in the fused RMS norm kernel, making it mutable and potentially vulnerable to accidental reassignment bugs.

The code review identifies one area where the performance tuning could be made more flexible, despite the well-implemented use of asynchronous DtoH copies, a dedicated CUDA stream, pinned memory, and a thread pool for I/O operations.

The `match` string passed to `pytest.raises` is incomplete, making the test insufficiently specific and potentially allowing unrelated exceptions with partially matching messages to pass the assertion.

The code review identifies that while the fix to conditionally disable pinned memory allocation is correct, the logic is duplicated across three locations and should be refactored into a shared helper function to improve maintainability and prevent future bugs.

The `kThreadsPerBlock` constant is set to 1024 without any explanatory comment, making it unclear whether the value was chosen for maximum occupancy, hardware limits, or some other configuration-driven reason, which could lead to future maintainers changing it inappropriately.

The caching for `InductorPass.uuid()` is implemented at the instance level, missing an opportunity to use a more memory-efficient and performant class-level cache that would prevent redundant hashing across multiple instances of the same pass class.

A `# type: ignore[return-value]` comment is used to silently suppress a type mismatch on the `architectures` property return value, which could mask real type errors and should instead be resolved with `typing.cast` or a more precise type annotation.

The `_last_avg_queue_time` attribute in `QueueTimeTracker` is initialized, assigned, and reset but never actually read or used in any logic, making it dead code that should be removed to avoid confusion with the misleading comment suggesting it drives decay calculations.

This pull request improves ROCm performance by implementing a dedicated `Mxfp4LinearMethod` that leverages AITER's Triton FP4 GEMM on ROCm and Marlin FP4 on CUDA, and enables shared expert fusion by default for MoE models, with a minor suggestion to improve comment clarity for maintainability.

The `sampler_workspace` parameter in the `forward` method is unused because the method body already accesses `self.sampler_workspace` directly, making the parameter redundant and potentially confusing — it should be removed along with its corresponding call sites in `gpu_model_runner.py`.

The pull request adds support for passing a HuggingFace authentication token (`hf_token`) through the vLLM engine configuration, enabling authenticated access when fetching model configurations via `PretrainedConfig.get_config_dict`.

The magic numbers `256` and `5` used in the `fixed_smem_size` calculation in `csrc/topk.cuh` lack named constants or explanatory comments, reducing code readability and making the intent of the shared memory size computation unclear.

A redundant dtype cast check exists after calling `self.dequant_func`, since both `dequant_mxfp4` and `dequant_mxfp6` already guarantee the output tensor is in the specified `compute_dtype`, making the extra operation unnecessary and adding avoidable overhead.

A redundant duplicate assignment sets `layer.moe_config.intermediate_size_per_partition = padded_intermediate` immediately after the same value is already assigned on the preceding line, which should be removed.

The pull request correctly fixes the handling of `default_chat_template_kwargs` in the `/v1/responses` API by propagating server-level defaults and per-request kwargs to prompt rendering and reasoning parser logic, with good test coverage, but contains a minor documentation issue.
The magic number `1` assigned to `next_n` lacks context or explanation, making the code harder to understand and maintain without a named constant or clarifying comment.

This pull request adds well-structured and thoroughly tested MXFP4 LoRA support for ROCm platforms via the Triton backend, including new CDNA4 device IDs for MI350X and MI355X, with no identified bugs or issues.

The code review introduces try/except error handling in `save_kv_layer` and `wait_for_save` to catch a `KeyError` in LMCache's layerwise storage mode, preventing the vLLM engine from crashing by logging a warning and continuing execution instead.

The benchmark code uses unexplained magic numbers `[0.5, 0.2, 0.8]` for `quantiles` values, which should be replaced with named constants or accompanied by comments to clarify their purpose in the benchmarking process.
The magic number `0.8` used to calculate `min_len` lacks explanation or a named constant, making the code harder to read and understand.

The hardcoded `BLOCK_THREADS` constant of `1024` in `csrc/topk.cuh` lacks any explanatory comment justifying why that specific value was chosen, making it unclear whether it is based on hardware limits, maximum occupancy, or some other configuration rationale.
A magic number `16` is used in `std::gcd(16 / sizeof(DType), max_len)` without explanation or a named constant, making the code's intent unclear and harder to maintain.

The assertion `assert manager_class is not None` in `kv_cache_registry.py` is redundant because the `manager_class` parameter is already type-hinted as a non-optional `type["SingleTypeKVCacheManager"]`, making the null check unnecessary.
The assertion `assert target_kv_cache_spec_cls is not None` is redundant because the parameter is already typed as `type["KVCacheSpec"]`, which excludes `None`, and can be safely removed.
The warmup size filtering logic iterates over `warmup_sizes` twice (once for invalid, once for valid sizes), which is inefficient and could be replaced with a single-pass partition into two lists.

The block size for `ROCM_AITER_UNIFIED_ATTN` is hardcoded in the test rather than being dynamically queried from the backend class, making the test brittle and prone to breaking if backend requirements change in the future.

The error handling logic for FlashInfer workspace initialization contains a redundant check for "multicasting" in the exception message, and should be simplified for better clarity and maintainability.
Duplicated shell command blocks that check for git-based packages in `common.txt` and `rocm.txt` should be refactored into a single loop to improve conciseness and maintainability.

The `const` qualifier was removed from the `out_norm` variable declaration, which is a minor style/safety regression since the variable is never modified after initialization and should remain `const` to enforce immutability and clarify intent.
The code review recommends simplifying the package exclusion list in the pre-commit configuration to improve maintainability of the new `uv pip compile` workflow added for ROCm test dependencies.
